verses.kjv: NOW these are the names of the children of Israel, which came into Egypt; every man and his household came with Jacob.
verses.bsb: These are the names of the sons of Israel who went to Egypt with Jacob, each with his family:

verses.kjv: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah,
verses.bsb: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah;

verses.kjv: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin,
verses.bsb: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin;

verses.kjv: Dan, and Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
verses.bsb: Dan and Naphtali;   Gad and Asher.

verses.kjv: And all the souls that came out of the loins of Jacob were seventy souls: for Joseph was in Egypt already.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Jacob numbered seventy in all, including Joseph, who was already in Egypt.

verses.kjv: And Joseph died, and all his brethren, and all that generation.
verses.bsb: Now Joseph and all his brothers and all that generation died,

verses.kjv: ¶ And the children of Israel were fruitful, and increased abundantly, and multiplied, and waxed exceeding mighty; and the land was filled with them.
verses.bsb: but the Israelites were fruitful and increased rapidly; they multiplied and became exceedingly numerous, so that the land was filled with them.

verses.kjv: Now there arose up a new king over Egypt, which knew not Joseph.
verses.bsb: Then a new king, who did not know Joseph, came to power in Egypt.

verses.kjv: And he said unto his people, Behold, the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we:
verses.bsb: “Look,” he said to his people, “the Israelites have become too numerous and too powerful for us.

verses.kjv: Come on, let us deal wisely with them; lest they multiply, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out any war, they join also unto our enemies, and fight against us, and so get them up out of the land.
verses.bsb: Come, let us deal shrewdly with them, or they will increase even more; and if a war breaks out, they may join our enemies, fight against us, and leave the country.”

verses.kjv: Therefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh treasure cities, Pithom and Raamses.
verses.bsb: So the Egyptians appointed taskmasters over the Israelites to oppress them with forced labor. As a result, they built Pithom and Rameses as store cities for Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: But the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and grew. And they were grieved because of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: But the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and flourished; so the Egyptians came to dread the Israelites.

verses.kjv: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour:
verses.bsb: They worked the Israelites ruthlessly

verses.kjv: And they made their lives bitter with hard bondage, in morter, and in brick, and in all manner of service in the field: all their service, wherein they made them serve, was with rigour.
verses.bsb: and made their lives bitter with hard labor in brick and mortar, and with all kinds of work in the fields. Every service they imposed was harsh.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the king of Egypt spake to the Hebrew midwives, of which the name of the one was Shiphrah, and the name of the other Puah:
verses.bsb: Then the king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, whose names were Shiphrah and Puah,

verses.kjv: And he said, When ye do the office of a midwife to the Hebrew women, and see them upon the stools; if it be a son, then ye shall kill him: but if it be a daughter, then she shall live.
verses.bsb: “When you help the Hebrew women give birth, observe them on the birthstools. If the child is a son, kill him; but if it is a daughter, let her live.”

verses.kjv: But the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the men children alive.
verses.bsb: The midwives, however, feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt had instructed; they let the boys live.

verses.kjv: And the king of Egypt called for the midwives, and said unto them, Why have ye done this thing, and have saved the men children alive?
verses.bsb: So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and asked them, “Why have you done this? Why have you let the boys live?”

verses.kjv: And the midwives said unto Pharaoh, Because the Hebrew women are not as the Egyptian women; for they are lively, and are delivered ere the midwives come in unto them.
verses.bsb: The midwives answered Pharaoh, “The Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women, for they are vigorous and give birth before a midwife arrives.”

verses.kjv: Therefore God dealt well with the midwives: and the people multiplied, and waxed very mighty.
verses.bsb: So God was good to the midwives, and the people multiplied and became even more numerous.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, because the midwives feared God, that he made them houses.
verses.bsb: And because the midwives feared God, He gave them families of their own.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh charged all his people, saying, Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river, and every daughter ye shall save alive.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh commanded all his people: “Every son born to the Hebrews you must throw into the Nile, but every daughter you may allow to live.”

verses.kjv: AND there went a man of the house of Levi, and took to wife a daughter of Levi.
verses.bsb: Now a man of the house of Levi married a daughter of Levi,

verses.kjv: And the woman conceived, and bare a son: and when she saw him that he was a goodly child, she hid him three months.
verses.bsb: and she conceived and gave birth to a son. When she saw that he was a beautiful child, she hid him for three months.

verses.kjv: And when she could not longer hide him, she took for him an ark of bulrushes, and daubed it with slime and with pitch, and put the child therein; and she laid it in the flags by the river’s brink.
verses.bsb: But when she could no longer hide him, she got him a papyrus basket and coated it with tar and pitch. Then she placed the child in the basket and set it among the reeds along the bank of the Nile.

verses.kjv: And his sister stood afar off, to wit what would be done to him.
verses.bsb: And his sister stood at a distance to see what would happen to him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the daughter of Pharaoh came down to wash herself at the river; and her maidens walked along by the river’s side; and when she saw the ark among the flags, she sent her maid to fetch it.
verses.bsb: Soon the daughter of Pharaoh went down to bathe in the Nile, and her attendants were walking along the riverbank. And when she saw the basket among the reeds, she sent her maidservant to retrieve it.

verses.kjv: And when she had opened it, she saw the child: and, behold, the babe wept. And she had compassion on him, and said, This is one of the Hebrews’ children.
verses.bsb: When she opened it, she saw the child, and behold, the little boy was crying. So she had compassion on him and said, “This is one of the Hebrew children.”

verses.kjv: Then said his sister to Pharaoh’s daughter, Shall I go and call to thee a nurse of the Hebrew women, that she may nurse the child for thee?
verses.bsb: Then his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, “Shall I go and call one of the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you?”

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, Go. And the maid went and called the child’s mother.
verses.bsb: “Go ahead,” Pharaoh’s daughter told her. And the girl went and called the boy’s mother.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh’s daughter said unto her, Take this child away, and nurse it for me, and I will give thee thy wages. And the woman took the child, and nursed it.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh’s daughter said to her, “Take this child and nurse him for me, and I will pay your wages.” So the woman took the boy and nursed him.

verses.kjv: And the child grew, and she brought him unto Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. And she called his name Moses: and she said, Because I drew him out of the water.
verses.bsb: When the child had grown older, she brought him to Pharaoh’s daughter, and he became her son. She named him Moses and explained, “I drew him out of the water.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in those days, when Moses was grown, that he went out unto his brethren, and looked on their burdens: and he spied an Egyptian smiting an Hebrew, one of his brethren.
verses.bsb: One day, after Moses had grown up, he went out to his own people and observed their hard labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his own people.

verses.kjv: And he looked this way and that way, and when he saw that there was no man, he slew the Egyptian, and hid him in the sand.
verses.bsb: After looking this way and that and seeing no one, he struck down the Egyptian and hid his body in the sand.

verses.kjv: And when he went out the second day, behold, two men of the Hebrews strove together: and he said to him that did the wrong, Wherefore smitest thou thy fellow?
verses.bsb: The next day Moses went out and saw two Hebrews fighting. He asked the one in the wrong, “Why are you attacking your companion?”

verses.kjv: And he said, Who made thee a prince and a judge over us? intendest thou to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? And Moses feared, and said, Surely this thing is known.
verses.bsb: But the man replied, “Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you planning to kill me as you killed the Egyptian?”  Then Moses was afraid and thought, “This thing I have done has surely become known.”

verses.kjv: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing, he sought to slay Moses. But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh, and dwelt in the land of Midian: and he sat down by a well.
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh heard about this matter, he sought to kill Moses. But Moses fled from Pharaoh and settled in the land of Midian, where he sat down beside a well.

verses.kjv: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters: and they came and drew water, and filled the troughs to water their father’s flock.
verses.bsb: Now the priest of Midian had seven daughters, and they came to draw water and fill the troughs to water their father’s flock.

verses.kjv: And the shepherds came and drove them away: but Moses stood up and helped them, and watered their flock.
verses.bsb: And when some shepherds came along and drove them away, Moses rose up to help them and watered their flock.

verses.kjv: And when they came to Reuel their father, he said, How is it that ye are come so soon to day?
verses.bsb: When the daughters returned to their father Reuel, he asked them, “Why have you returned so early today?”

verses.kjv: And they said, An Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and also drew water enough for us, and watered the flock.
verses.bsb: “An Egyptian rescued us from the shepherds,” they replied. “He even drew water for us and watered the flock.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto his daughters, And where is he? why is it that ye have left the man? call him, that he may eat bread.
verses.bsb: “So where is he?” their father asked. “Why did you leave the man behind? Invite him to have something to eat.”

verses.kjv: And Moses was content to dwell with the man: and he gave Moses Zipporah his daughter.
verses.bsb: Moses agreed to stay with the man, and he gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses in marriage.

verses.kjv: And she bare him a son, and he called his name Gershom: for he said, I have been a stranger in a strange land.
verses.bsb: And she gave birth to a son, and Moses named him Gershom, saying, “I have become a foreigner in a foreign land.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in process of time, that the king of Egypt died: and the children of Israel sighed by reason of the bondage, and they cried, and their cry came up unto God by reason of the bondage.
verses.bsb: After a long time, the king of Egypt died. The Israelites groaned and cried out under their burden of slavery, and their cry for deliverance from bondage ascended to God.

verses.kjv: And God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.
verses.bsb: So God heard their groaning, and He remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

verses.kjv: And God looked upon the children of Israel, and God had respect unto them.
verses.bsb: God saw the Israelites and took notice.

verses.kjv: NOW Moses kept the flock of Jethro his father in law, the priest of Midian: and he led the flock to the backside of the desert, and came to the mountain of God, even to Horeb.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Moses was shepherding the flock of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian. He led the flock to the far side of the wilderness and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.

verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush: and he looked, and, behold, the bush burned with fire, and the bush was not consumed.
verses.bsb: There the angel of the LORD appeared to him in a blazing fire from within a bush. Moses saw the bush ablaze with fire, but it was not consumed.

verses.kjv: And Moses said, I will now turn aside, and see this great sight, why the bush is not burnt.
verses.bsb: So Moses thought, “I must go over and see this marvelous sight. Why is the bush not burning up?”

verses.kjv: And when the Lord saw that he turned aside to see, God called unto him out of the midst of the bush, and said, Moses, Moses. And he said, Here am I.
verses.bsb: When the LORD saw that he had gone over to look, God called out to him from within the bush, “Moses, Moses!”  “Here I am,” he answered.

verses.kjv: And he said, Draw not nigh hither: put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.
verses.bsb: “Do not come any closer,” God said. “Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.”

verses.kjv: Moreover he said, I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face; for he was afraid to look upon God.
verses.bsb: Then He said, “I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”  At this, Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said, I have surely seen the affliction of my people which are in Egypt, and have heard their cry by reason of their taskmasters; for I know their sorrows;
verses.bsb: The LORD said, “I have indeed seen the affliction of My people in Egypt. I have heard them crying out because of their oppressors, and I am aware of their sufferings.

verses.kjv: And I am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians, and to bring them up out of that land unto a good land and a large, unto a land flowing with milk and honey; unto the place of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
verses.bsb: I have come down to rescue them from the hand of the Egyptians and to bring them up out of that land to a good and spacious land, a land flowing with milk and honey—the home of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.

verses.kjv: Now therefore, behold, the cry of the children of Israel is come unto me: and I have also seen the oppression wherewith the Egyptians oppress them.
verses.bsb: And now the cry of the Israelites has reached Me, and I have seen how severely the Egyptians are oppressing them.

verses.kjv: Come now therefore, and I will send thee unto Pharaoh, that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of Israel out of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Therefore, go! I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring My people the Israelites out of Egypt.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto God, Who am I, that I should go unto Pharaoh, and that I should bring forth the children of Israel out of Egypt?
verses.bsb: But Moses asked God, “Who am I, that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?”

verses.kjv: And he said, Certainly I will be with thee; and this shall be a token unto thee, that I have sent thee: When thou hast brought forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall serve God upon this mountain.
verses.bsb: “I will surely be with you,” God said, “and this will be the sign to you that I have sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, all of you will worship God on this mountain.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto God, Behold, when I come unto the children of Israel, and shall say unto them, The God of your fathers hath sent me unto you; and they shall say to me, What is his name? what shall I say unto them?
verses.bsb: Then Moses asked God, “Suppose I go to the Israelites and say to them, ‘The God of your fathers has sent me to you,’ and they ask me, ‘What is His name?’ What should I tell them?”

verses.kjv: And God said unto Moses, I AM THAT I AM: and he said, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, I AM hath sent me unto you.
verses.bsb: God said to Moses, “I AM WHO I AM. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: ‘I AM has sent me to you.’”

verses.kjv: And God said moreover unto Moses, Thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath sent me unto you: this is my name for ever, and this is my memorial unto all generations.
verses.bsb: God also told Moses, “Say to the Israelites, ‘The LORD, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob—has sent me to you.’ This is My name forever, and this is how I am to be remembered in every generation.

verses.kjv: Go, and gather the elders of Israel together, and say unto them, The Lord God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, appeared unto me, saying, I have surely visited you, and seen that which is done to you in Egypt:
verses.bsb: Go, assemble the elders of Israel and say to them, ‘The LORD, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—has appeared to me and said: I have surely attended to you and have seen what has been done to you in Egypt.

verses.kjv: And I have said, I will bring you up out of the affliction of Egypt unto the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, unto a land flowing with milk and honey.
verses.bsb: And I have promised to bring you up out of your affliction in Egypt, into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites—a land flowing with milk and honey.’

verses.kjv: And they shall hearken to thy voice: and thou shalt come, thou and the elders of Israel, unto the king of Egypt, and ye shall say unto him, The Lord God of the Hebrews hath met with us: and now let us go, we beseech thee, three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the Lord our God.
verses.bsb: The elders of Israel will listen to what you say, and you must go with them to the king of Egypt and tell him, ‘The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Now please let us take a three-day journey into the wilderness, so that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.’

verses.kjv: ¶ And I am sure that the king of Egypt will not let you go, no, not by a mighty hand.
verses.bsb: But I know that the king of Egypt will not allow you to go unless a mighty hand compels him.

verses.kjv: And I will stretch out my hand, and smite Egypt with all my wonders which I will do in the midst thereof: and after that he will let you go.
verses.bsb: So I will stretch out My hand and strike the Egyptians with all the wonders I will perform among them. And after that, he will release you.

verses.kjv: And I will give this people favour in the sight of the Egyptians: and it shall come to pass, that, when ye go, ye shall not go empty:
verses.bsb: And I will grant this people such favor in the sight of the Egyptians that when you leave, you will not go away empty-handed.

verses.kjv: But every woman shall borrow of her neighbour, and of her that sojourneth in her house, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment: and ye shall put them upon your sons, and upon your daughters; and ye shall spoil the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: Every woman shall ask her neighbor and any woman staying in her house for silver and gold jewelry and clothing, and you will put them on your sons and daughters. So you will plunder the Egyptians.”

verses.kjv: AND Moses answered and said, But, behold, they will not believe me, nor hearken unto my voice: for they will say, The Lord hath not appeared unto thee.
verses.bsb: Then Moses answered, “What if they do not believe me or listen to my voice? For they may say, ‘The LORD has not appeared to you.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, What is that in thine hand? And he said, A rod.
verses.bsb: And the LORD asked him, “What is that in your hand?”  “A staff,” he replied.

verses.kjv: And he said, Cast it on the ground. And he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from before it.
verses.bsb: “Throw it on the ground,” said the LORD. So Moses threw it on the ground, and it became a snake, and he ran from it.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Put forth thine hand, and take it by the tail. And he put forth his hand, and caught it, and it became a rod in his hand:
verses.bsb: “Stretch out your hand and grab it by the tail,” the LORD said to Moses, who reached out his hand and caught the snake, and it turned back into a staff in his hand.

verses.kjv: That they may believe that the Lord God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.
verses.bsb: “This is so that they may believe that the LORD, the God of their fathers—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob—has appeared to you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said furthermore unto him, Put now thine hand into thy bosom. And he put his hand into his bosom: and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous as snow.
verses.bsb: Furthermore, the LORD said to Moses, “Put your hand inside your cloak.” So he put his hand inside his cloak, and when he took it out, his hand was leprous, white as snow.

verses.kjv: And he said, Put thine hand into thy bosom again. And he put his hand into his bosom again; and plucked it out of his bosom, and, behold, it was turned again as his other flesh.
verses.bsb: “Put your hand back inside your cloak,” said the LORD.  So Moses put his hand back inside his cloak, and when he took it out, it was restored, like the rest of his skin.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe thee, neither hearken to the voice of the first sign, that they will believe the voice of the latter sign.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said, “If they refuse to believe you or heed the witness of the first sign, they may believe that of the second.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe also these two signs, neither hearken unto thy voice, that thou shalt take of the water of the river, and pour it upon the dry land: and the water which thou takest out of the river shall become blood upon the dry land.
verses.bsb: But if they do not believe even these two signs or listen to your voice, take some water from the Nile and pour it on the dry ground. Then the water you take from the Nile will become blood on the ground.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the Lord, O my Lord, I am not eloquent, neither heretofore, nor since thou hast spoken unto thy servant: but I am slow of speech, and of a slow tongue.
verses.bsb: “Please, Lord,” Moses replied, “I have never been eloquent, neither in the past nor since You have spoken to Your servant, for I am slow of speech and tongue.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Who hath made man’s mouth? or who maketh the dumb, or deaf, or the seeing, or the blind? have not I the Lord?
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to him, “Who gave man his mouth? Or who makes the mute or the deaf, the sighted or the blind? Is it not I, the LORD?

verses.kjv: Now therefore go, and I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee what thou shalt say.
verses.bsb: Now go! I will help you as you speak, and I will teach you what to say.”

verses.kjv: And he said, O my Lord, send, I pray thee, by the hand of him whom thou wilt send.
verses.bsb: But Moses replied, “Please, Lord, send someone else.”

verses.kjv: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses, and he said, Is not Aaron the Levite thy brother? I know that he can speak well. And also, behold, he cometh forth to meet thee: and when he seeth thee, he will be glad in his heart.
verses.bsb: Then the anger of the LORD burned against Moses, and He said, “Is not Aaron the Levite your brother? I know that he can speak well, and he is now on his way to meet you. When he sees you, he will be glad in his heart.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
verses.bsb: You are to speak to him and put the words in his mouth. I will help both of you to speak, and I will teach you what to do.

verses.kjv: And he shall be thy spokesman unto the people: and he shall be, even he shall be to thee instead of a mouth, and thou shalt be to him instead of God.
verses.bsb: He will speak to the people for you. He will be your spokesman, and it will be as if you were God to him.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take this rod in thine hand, wherewith thou shalt do signs.
verses.bsb: But take this staff in your hand so you can perform signs with it.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses went and returned to Jethro his father in law, and said unto him, Let me go, I pray thee, and return unto my brethren which are in Egypt, and see whether they be yet alive. And Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.
verses.bsb: Then Moses went back to his father-in-law Jethro and said to him, “Please let me return to my brothers in Egypt to see if they are still alive.”  “Go in peace,” Jethro replied.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses in Midian, Go, return into Egypt: for all the men are dead which sought thy life.
verses.bsb: Now the LORD had said to Moses in Midian, “Go back to Egypt, for all the men who sought to kill you are dead.”

verses.kjv: And Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them upon an ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt: and Moses took the rod of God in his hand.
verses.bsb: So Moses took his wife and sons, put them on a donkey, and headed back to Egypt. And he took the staff of God in his hand.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, When thou goest to return into Egypt, see that thou do all those wonders before Pharaoh, which I have put in thine hand: but I will harden his heart, that he shall not let the people go.
verses.bsb: The LORD instructed Moses, “When you go back to Egypt, see that you perform before Pharaoh all the wonders that I have put within your power. But I will harden his heart so that he will not let the people go.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt say unto Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord, Israel is my son, even my firstborn:
verses.bsb: Then tell Pharaoh that this is what the LORD says: ‘Israel is My firstborn son,

verses.kjv: And I say unto thee, Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
verses.bsb: and I told you to let My son go so that he may worship Me. But since you have refused to let him go, behold, I will kill your firstborn son!’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass by the way in the inn, that the Lord met him, and sought to kill him.
verses.bsb: Now at a lodging place along the way, the LORD met Moses and was about to kill him.

verses.kjv: Then Zipporah took a sharp stone, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet, and said, Surely a bloody husband art thou to me.
verses.bsb: But Zipporah took a flint knife, cut off her son’s foreskin, and touched it to Moses’ feet. “Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me,” she said.

verses.kjv: So he let him go: then she said, A bloody husband thou art, because of the circumcision.
verses.bsb: So the LORD let him alone. (When she said, “bridegroom of blood,” she was referring to the circumcision.)

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said to Aaron, Go into the wilderness to meet Moses. And he went, and met him in the mount of God, and kissed him.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the LORD had said to Aaron, “Go and meet Moses in the wilderness.” So he went and met Moses at the mountain of God and kissed him.

verses.kjv: And Moses told Aaron all the words of the Lord who had sent him, and all the signs which he had commanded him.
verses.bsb: And Moses told Aaron everything the LORD had sent him to say, and all the signs He had commanded him to perform.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel:
verses.bsb: Then Moses and Aaron went and assembled all the elders of the Israelites,

verses.kjv: And Aaron spake all the words which the Lord had spoken unto Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.
verses.bsb: and Aaron relayed everything the LORD had said to Moses.  And Moses performed the signs before the people,

verses.kjv: And the people believed: and when they heard that the Lord had visited the children of Israel, and that he had looked upon their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.
verses.bsb: and they believed. And when they heard that the LORD had attended to the Israelites and had seen their affliction, they bowed down and worshiped.

verses.kjv: AND afterward Moses and Aaron went in, and told Pharaoh, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Let my people go, that they may hold a feast unto me in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: After that, Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, “This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: ‘Let My people go, so that they may hold a feast to Me in the wilderness.’”

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said, Who is the Lord, that I should obey his voice to let Israel go? I know not the Lord, neither will I let Israel go.
verses.bsb: But Pharaoh replied, “Who is the LORD that I should obey His voice and let Israel go? I do not know the LORD, and I will not let Israel go.”

verses.kjv: And they said, The God of the Hebrews hath met with us: let us go, we pray thee, three days’ journey into the desert, and sacrifice unto the Lord our God; lest he fall upon us with pestilence, or with the sword.
verses.bsb: “The God of the Hebrews has met with us,” they answered. “Please let us go on a three-day journey into the wilderness to sacrifice to the LORD our God, or He may strike us with plagues or with the sword.”

verses.kjv: And the king of Egypt said unto them, Wherefore do ye, Moses and Aaron, let the people from their works? get you unto your burdens.
verses.bsb: But the king of Egypt said to them, “Moses and Aaron, why do you draw the people away from their work? Get back to your labor!”

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said, Behold, the people of the land now are many, and ye make them rest from their burdens.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh also said, “Look, the people of the land are now numerous, and you would be stopping them from their labor.”

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh commanded the same day the taskmasters of the people, and their officers, saying,
verses.bsb: That same day Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters of the people and their foremen:

verses.kjv: Ye shall no more give the people straw to make brick, as heretofore: let them go and gather straw for themselves.
verses.bsb: “You shall no longer supply the people with straw for making bricks. They must go and gather their own straw.

verses.kjv: And the tale of the bricks, which they did make heretofore, ye shall lay upon them; ye shall not diminish ought thereof: for they be idle; therefore they cry, saying, Let us go and sacrifice to our God.
verses.bsb: But require of them the same quota of bricks as before; do not reduce it. For they are lazy; that is why they are crying out, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to our God.’

verses.kjv: Let there more work be laid upon the men, that they may labour therein; and let them not regard vain words.
verses.bsb: Make the work harder on the men so they will be occupied and pay no attention to these lies.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the taskmasters of the people went out, and their officers, and they spake to the people, saying, Thus saith Pharaoh, I will not give you straw.
verses.bsb: So the taskmasters and foremen of the people went out and said to them, “This is what Pharaoh says: ‘I am no longer giving you straw.

verses.kjv: Go ye, get you straw where ye can find it: yet not ought of your work shall be diminished.
verses.bsb: Go and get your own straw wherever you can find it; but your workload will in no way be reduced.’”

verses.kjv: So the people were scattered abroad throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble instead of straw.
verses.bsb: So the people scattered all over the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.

verses.kjv: And the taskmasters hasted them, saying, Fulfil your works, your daily tasks, as when there was straw.
verses.bsb: The taskmasters kept pressing them, saying, “Fulfill your quota each day, just as you did when straw was provided.”

verses.kjv: And the officers of the children of Israel, which Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over them, were beaten, and demanded, Wherefore have ye not fulfilled your task in making brick both yesterday and to day, as heretofore?
verses.bsb: Then the Israelite foremen, whom Pharaoh’s taskmasters had set over the people, were beaten and asked, “Why have you not fulfilled your quota of bricks yesterday or today, as you did before?”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then the officers of the children of Israel came and cried unto Pharaoh, saying, Wherefore dealest thou thus with thy servants?
verses.bsb: So the Israelite foremen went and appealed to Pharaoh: “Why are you treating your servants this way?

verses.kjv: There is no straw given unto thy servants, and they say to us, Make brick: and, behold, thy servants are beaten; but the fault is in thine own people.
verses.bsb: No straw has been given to your servants, yet we are told, ‘Make bricks!’ Look, your servants are being beaten, but the fault is with your own people.”

verses.kjv: But he said, Ye are idle, ye are idle: therefore ye say, Let us go and do sacrifice to the Lord.
verses.bsb: “You are slackers!” Pharaoh replied. “Slackers! That is why you keep saying, ‘Let us go and sacrifice to the LORD.’

verses.kjv: Go therefore now, and work; for there shall no straw be given you, yet shall ye deliver the tale of bricks.
verses.bsb: Now get to work. You will be given no straw, yet you must deliver the full quota of bricks.”

verses.kjv: And the officers of the children of Israel did see that they were in evil case, after it was said, Ye shall not minish ought from your bricks of your daily task.
verses.bsb: The Israelite foremen realized they were in trouble when they were told, “You must not reduce your daily quota of bricks.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they met Moses and Aaron, who stood in the way, as they came forth from Pharaoh:
verses.bsb: When they left Pharaoh, they confronted Moses and Aaron, who stood waiting to meet them.

verses.kjv: And they said unto them, The Lord look upon you, and judge; because ye have made our savour to be abhorred in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of his servants, to put a sword in their hand to slay us.
verses.bsb: “May the LORD look upon you and judge you,” the foremen said, “for you have made us a stench before Pharaoh and his officials; you have placed in their hand a sword to kill us!”

verses.kjv: And Moses returned unto the Lord, and said, Lord, wherefore hast thou so evil entreated this people? why is it that thou hast sent me?
verses.bsb: So Moses returned to the LORD and asked, “Lord, why have You brought trouble upon this people? Is this why You sent me?

verses.kjv: For since I came to Pharaoh to speak in thy name, he hath done evil to this people; neither hast thou delivered thy people at all.
verses.bsb: Ever since I went to Pharaoh to speak in Your name, he has brought trouble on this people, and You have not delivered Your people in any way.”

verses.kjv: THEN the Lord said unto Moses, Now shalt thou see what I will do to Pharaoh: for with a strong hand shall he let them go, and with a strong hand shall he drive them out of his land.
verses.bsb: But the LORD said to Moses, “Now you will see what I will do to Pharaoh, for because of My mighty hand he will let the people go; because of My strong hand he will drive them out of his land.”

verses.kjv: And God spake unto Moses, and said unto him, I am the Lord:
verses.bsb: God also told Moses, “I am the LORD.

verses.kjv: And I appeared unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob, by the name of God Almighty, but by my name JEHOVAH was I not known to them.
verses.bsb: I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as God Almighty, but by My name the LORD I did not make Myself known to them.

verses.kjv: And I have also established my covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their pilgrimage, wherein they were strangers.
verses.bsb: I also established My covenant with them to give them the land of Canaan, the land where they lived as foreigners.

verses.kjv: And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians keep in bondage; and I have remembered my covenant.
verses.bsb: Furthermore, I have heard the groaning of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians are enslaving, and I have remembered My covenant.

verses.kjv: Wherefore say unto the children of Israel, I am the Lord, and I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and I will rid you out of their bondage, and I will redeem you with a stretched out arm, and with great judgments:
verses.bsb: Therefore tell the Israelites: ‘I am the LORD, and I will bring you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians and deliver you from their bondage. I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with mighty acts of judgment.

verses.kjv: And I will take you to me for a people, and I will be to you a God: and ye shall know that I am the Lord your God, which bringeth you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: I will take you as My own people, and I will be your God. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God, who brought you out from under the yoke of the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: And I will bring you in unto the land, concerning the which I did swear to give it to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it you for an heritage: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: And I will bring you into the land that I swore to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I will give it to you as a possession. I am the LORD!’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses spake so unto the children of Israel: but they hearkened not unto Moses for anguish of spirit, and for cruel bondage.
verses.bsb: Moses relayed this message to the Israelites, but on account of their broken spirit and cruel bondage, they did not listen to him.

verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: So the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Go in, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.
verses.bsb: “Go and tell Pharaoh king of Egypt to let the Israelites go out of his land.”

verses.kjv: And Moses spake before the Lord, saying, Behold, the children of Israel have not hearkened unto me; how then shall Pharaoh hear me, who am of uncircumcised lips?
verses.bsb: But in the LORD’s presence Moses replied, “If the Israelites will not listen to me, then why would Pharaoh listen to me, since I am unskilled in speech?”

verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel, and unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron and gave them a charge concerning both the Israelites and Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: ¶ These be the heads of their fathers’ houses: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel; Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi: these be the families of Reuben.
verses.bsb: These were the heads of their fathers’ houses:  The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, were Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi. These were the clans of Reuben.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Simeon; Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul the son of a Canaanitish woman: these are the families of Simeon.
verses.bsb: The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman. These were the clans of Simeon.

verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations; Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari: and the years of the life of Levi were an hundred thirty and seven years.
verses.bsb: These were the names of the sons of Levi according to their records: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. Levi lived 137 years.

verses.kjv: The sons of Gershon; Libni, and Shimi, according to their families.
verses.bsb: The sons of Gershon were Libni and Shimei, by their clans.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Kohath; Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel: and the years of the life of Kohath were an hundred thirty and three years.
verses.bsb: The sons of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel. Kohath lived 133 years.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Merari; Mahali and Mushi: these are the families of Levi according to their generations.
verses.bsb: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi.   These were the clans of the Levites according to their records.

verses.kjv: And Amram took him Jochebed his father’s sister to wife; and she bare him Aaron and Moses: and the years of the life of Amram were an hundred and thirty and seven years.
verses.bsb: And Amram married his father’s sister Jochebed, and she bore him Aaron and Moses. Amram lived 137 years.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the sons of Izhar; Korah, and Nepheg, and Zichri.
verses.bsb: The sons of Izhar were Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Uzziel; Mishael, and Elzaphan, and Zithri.
verses.bsb: The sons of Uzziel were Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.

verses.kjv: And Aaron took him Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Naashon, to wife; and she bare him Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.bsb: And Aaron married Elisheba, the daughter of Amminadab and sister of Nahshon, and she bore him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Korah; Assir, and Elkanah, and Abiasaph: these are the families of the Korhites.
verses.bsb: The sons of Korah were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These were the clans of the Korahites.

verses.kjv: And Eleazar Aaron’s son took him one of the daughters of Putiel to wife; and she bare him Phinehas: these are the heads of the fathers of the Levites according to their families.
verses.bsb: Aaron’s son Eleazar married one of the daughters of Putiel, and she bore him Phinehas.   These were the heads of the Levite families by their clans.

verses.kjv: These are that Aaron and Moses, to whom the Lord said, Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their armies.
verses.bsb: It was this Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said, “Bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt by their divisions.”

verses.kjv: These are they which spake to Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt: these are that Moses and Aaron.
verses.bsb: Moses and Aaron were the ones who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt in order to bring the Israelites out of Egypt.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the day when the Lord spake unto Moses in the land of Egypt,
verses.bsb: Now on the day that the LORD spoke to Moses in Egypt,

verses.kjv: That the Lord spake unto Moses, saying, I am the Lord: speak thou unto Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I say unto thee.
verses.bsb: He said to him, “I am the LORD; tell Pharaoh king of Egypt everything I say to you.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said before the Lord, Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips, and how shall Pharaoh hearken unto me?
verses.bsb: But in the LORD’s presence Moses replied, “Since I am unskilled in speech, why would Pharaoh listen to me?”

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, See, I have made thee a god to Pharaoh: and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.
verses.bsb: The LORD answered Moses, “See, I have made you like God to Pharaoh, and your brother Aaron will be your prophet.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt speak all that I command thee: and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he send the children of Israel out of his land.
verses.bsb: You are to speak all that I command you, and your brother Aaron is to tell Pharaoh to let the Israelites go out of his land.

verses.kjv: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: But I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and though I will multiply My signs and wonders in the land of Egypt,

verses.kjv: But Pharaoh shall not hearken unto you, that I may lay my hand upon Egypt, and bring forth mine armies, and my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh will not listen to you.  Then I will lay My hand on Egypt, and by mighty acts of judgment I will bring the divisions of My people the Israelites out of the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I stretch forth mine hand upon Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.
verses.bsb: And the Egyptians will know that I am the LORD, when I stretch out My hand against Egypt and bring the Israelites out from among them.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron did as the Lord commanded them, so did they.
verses.bsb: So Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD had commanded them.

verses.kjv: And Moses was fourscore years old, and Aaron fourscore and three years old, when they spake unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: Moses was eighty years old and Aaron was eighty-three when they spoke to Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
verses.bsb: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,

verses.kjv: When Pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying, Shew a miracle for you: then thou shalt say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and cast it before Pharaoh, and it shall become a serpent.
verses.bsb: “When Pharaoh tells you, ‘Perform a miracle,’ you are to say to Aaron, ‘Take your staff and throw it down before Pharaoh,’ and it will become a serpent.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and they did so as the Lord had commanded: and Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh, and before his servants, and it became a serpent.
verses.bsb: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did just as the LORD had commanded. Aaron threw his staff down before Pharaoh and his officials, and it became a serpent.

verses.kjv: Then Pharaoh also called the wise men and the sorcerers: now the magicians of Egypt, they also did in like manner with their enchantments.
verses.bsb: But Pharaoh called the wise men and sorcerers and magicians of Egypt, and they also did the same things by their magic arts.

verses.kjv: For they cast down every man his rod, and they became serpents: but Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.
verses.bsb: Each one threw down his staff, and it became a serpent. But Aaron’s staff swallowed up the other staffs.

verses.kjv: And he hardened Pharaoh’s heart, that he hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
verses.bsb: Still, Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Pharaoh’s heart is hardened, he refuseth to let the people go.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Pharaoh’s heart is unyielding; he refuses to let the people go.

verses.kjv: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning; lo, he goeth out unto the water; and thou shalt stand by the river’s brink against he come; and the rod which was turned to a serpent shalt thou take in thine hand.
verses.bsb: Go to Pharaoh in the morning as you see him walking out to the water. Wait on the bank of the Nile to meet him, and take in your hand the staff that was changed into a snake.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt say unto him, The Lord God of the Hebrews hath sent me unto thee, saying, Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness: and, behold, hitherto thou wouldest not hear.
verses.bsb: Then say to him, ‘The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, has sent me to tell you: Let My people go, so that they may worship Me in the wilderness. But you have not listened until now.

verses.kjv: Thus saith the Lord, In this thou shalt know that I am the Lord: behold, I will smite with the rod that is in mine hand upon the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.
verses.bsb: This is what the LORD says: By this you will know that I am the LORD. Behold, with the staff in my hand I will strike the water of the Nile, and it will turn to blood.

verses.kjv: And the fish that is in the river shall die, and the river shall stink; and the Egyptians shall lothe to drink of the water of the river.
verses.bsb: The fish in the Nile will die, the river will stink, and the Egyptians will be unable to drink its water.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and stretch out thine hand upon the waters of Egypt, upon their streams, upon their rivers, and upon their ponds, and upon all their pools of water, that they may become blood; and that there may be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood, and in vessels of stone.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Take your staff and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt—over their rivers and canals and ponds and reservoirs—that they may become blood.’ There will be blood throughout the land of Egypt, even in the vessels of wood and stone.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron did so, as the Lord commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.
verses.bsb: Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD had commanded; in the presence of Pharaoh and his officials, Aaron raised the staff and struck the water of the Nile, and all the water was turned to blood.

verses.kjv: And the fish that was in the river died; and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink of the water of the river; and there was blood throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: The fish in the Nile died, and the river smelled so bad that the Egyptians could not drink its water. And there was blood throughout the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And the magicians of Egypt did so with their enchantments: and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, neither did he hearken unto them; as the Lord had said.
verses.bsb: But the magicians of Egypt did the same things by their magic arts. So Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not listen to Moses and Aaron, just as the LORD had said.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, neither did he set his heart to this also.
verses.bsb: Instead, Pharaoh turned around, went into his palace, and did not take any of this to heart.

verses.kjv: And all the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.
verses.bsb: So all the Egyptians dug around the Nile for water to drink, because they could not drink the water from the river.

verses.kjv: And seven days were fulfilled, after that the Lord had smitten the river.
verses.bsb: And seven full days passed after the LORD had struck the Nile.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, Go unto Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go to Pharaoh and tell him that this is what the LORD says: ‘Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.

verses.kjv: And if thou refuse to let them go, behold, I will smite all thy borders with frogs:
verses.bsb: But if you refuse to let them go, I will plague your whole country with frogs.

verses.kjv: And the river shall bring forth frogs abundantly, which shall go up and come into thine house, and into thy bedchamber, and upon thy bed, and into the house of thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thine ovens, and into thy kneadingtroughs:
verses.bsb: The Nile will teem with frogs, and they will come into your palace and up to your bedroom and onto your bed, into the houses of your officials and your people, and into your ovens and kneading bowls.

verses.kjv: And the frogs shall come up both on thee, and upon thy people, and upon all thy servants.
verses.bsb: The frogs will come up on you and your people and all your officials.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch forth thine hand with thy rod over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up upon the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Stretch out your hand with your staff over the rivers and canals and ponds, and cause the frogs to come up onto the land of Egypt.’”

verses.kjv: And Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; and the frogs came up, and covered the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And the magicians did so with their enchantments, and brought up frogs upon the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: But the magicians did the same thing by their magic arts, and they also brought frogs up onto the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, Intreat the Lord, that he may take away the frogs from me, and from my people; and I will let the people go, that they may do sacrifice unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “Pray to the LORD to take the frogs away from me and my people. Then I will let your people go, that they may sacrifice to the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto Pharaoh, Glory over me: when shall I intreat for thee, and for thy servants, and for thy people, to destroy the frogs from thee and thy houses, that they may remain in the river only?
verses.bsb: Moses said to Pharaoh, “You may have the honor over me. When shall I pray for you and your officials and your people that the frogs (except for those in the Nile) may be taken away from you and your houses?”

verses.kjv: And he said, To morrow. And he said, Be it according to thy word: that thou mayest know that there is none like unto the Lord our God.
verses.bsb: “Tomorrow,” Pharaoh answered.  “May it be as you say,” Moses replied, “so that you may know that there is no one like the LORD our God.

verses.kjv: And the frogs shall depart from thee, and from thy houses, and from thy servants, and from thy people; they shall remain in the river only.
verses.bsb: The frogs will depart from you and your houses and your officials and your people; they will remain only in the Nile.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh: and Moses cried unto the Lord because of the frogs which he had brought against Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: After Moses and Aaron had left Pharaoh, Moses cried out to the LORD for help with the frogs that He had brought against Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the villages, and out of the fields.
verses.bsb: And the LORD did as Moses requested, and the frogs in the houses, the courtyards, and the fields died.

verses.kjv: And they gathered them together upon heaps: and the land stank.
verses.bsb: They were piled into countless heaps, and there was a terrible stench in the land.

verses.kjv: But when Pharaoh saw that there was respite, he hardened his heart, and hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh saw that there was relief, however, he hardened his heart and would not listen to Moses and Aaron, just as the LORD had said.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Stretch out thy rod, and smite the dust of the land, that it may become lice throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Tell Aaron, ‘Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, that it may turn into swarms of gnats throughout the land of Egypt.’”

verses.kjv: And they did so; for Aaron stretched out his hand with his rod, and smote the dust of the earth, and it became lice in man, and in beast; all the dust of the land became lice throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: This they did, and when Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth, gnats came upon man and beast. All the dust of the earth turned into gnats throughout the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And the magicians did so with their enchantments to bring forth lice, but they could not: so there were lice upon man, and upon beast.
verses.bsb: The magicians tried to produce gnats using their magic arts, but they could not. And the gnats remained on man and beast.

verses.kjv: Then the magicians said unto Pharaoh, This is the finger of God: and Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had said.
verses.bsb: “This is the finger of God,” the magicians said to Pharaoh. But Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh; lo, he cometh forth to the water; and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Get up early in the morning, and when Pharaoh goes out to the water, stand before him and tell him that this is what the LORD says: ‘Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.

verses.kjv: Else, if thou wilt not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies upon thee, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people, and into thy houses: and the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of swarms of flies, and also the ground whereon they are.
verses.bsb: But if you will not let My people go, I will send swarms of flies upon you and your officials and your people and your houses. The houses of the Egyptians and even the ground where they stand will be full of flies.

verses.kjv: And I will sever in that day the land of Goshen, in which my people dwell, that no swarms of flies shall be there; to the end thou mayest know that I am the Lord in the midst of the earth.
verses.bsb: But on that day I will give special treatment to the land of Goshen, where My people live; no swarms of flies will be found there. In this way you will know that I, the LORD, am in the land.

verses.kjv: And I will put a division between my people and thy people: to morrow shall this sign be.
verses.bsb: I will make a distinction between My people and your people. This sign will take place tomorrow.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord did so; and there came a grievous swarm of flies into the house of Pharaoh, and into his servants’ houses, and into all the land of Egypt: the land was corrupted by reason of the swarm of flies.
verses.bsb: And the LORD did so. Thick swarms of flies poured into Pharaoh’s palace and into the houses of his officials. Throughout Egypt the land was ruined by swarms of flies.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Pharaoh called for Moses and for Aaron, and said, Go ye, sacrifice to your God in the land.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “Go, sacrifice to your God within this land.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, It is not meet so to do; for we shall sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians to the Lord our God: lo, shall we sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, and will they not stone us?
verses.bsb: But Moses replied, “It would not be right to do that, because the sacrifices we offer to the LORD our God would be detestable to the Egyptians. If we offer sacrifices that are detestable before the Egyptians, will they not stone us?

verses.kjv: We will go three days’ journey into the wilderness, and sacrifice to the Lord our God, as he shall command us.
verses.bsb: We must make a three-day journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God as He commands us.”

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said, I will let you go, that ye may sacrifice to the Lord your God in the wilderness; only ye shall not go very far away: intreat for me.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh answered, “I will let you go and sacrifice to the LORD your God in the wilderness, but you must not go very far. Now pray for me.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Behold, I go out from thee, and I will intreat the Lord that the swarms of flies may depart from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, to morrow: but let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully any more in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the Lord.
verses.bsb: “As soon as I leave you,” Moses said, “I will pray to the LORD, so that tomorrow the swarms of flies will depart from Pharaoh and his officials and his people. But Pharaoh must not act deceitfully again by refusing to let the people go and sacrifice to the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And Moses went out from Pharaoh, and intreated the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then Moses left Pharaoh and prayed to the LORD,

verses.kjv: And the Lord did according to the word of Moses; and he removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; there remained not one.
verses.bsb: and the LORD did as Moses requested. He removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh and his officials and his people; not one fly remained.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh hardened his heart at this time also, neither would he let the people go.
verses.bsb: But Pharaoh hardened his heart this time as well, and he would not let the people go.

verses.kjv: THEN the Lord said unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh, and tell him, Thus saith the Lord God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go to Pharaoh and tell him that this is what the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.

verses.kjv: For if thou refuse to let them go, and wilt hold them still,
verses.bsb: But if you continue to restrain them and refuse to let them go,

verses.kjv: Behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thy cattle which is in the field, upon the horses, upon the asses, upon the camels, upon the oxen, and upon the sheep: there shall be a very grievous murrain.
verses.bsb: then the hand of the LORD will bring a severe plague on your livestock in the field—on your horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks.

verses.kjv: And the Lord shall sever between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt: and there shall nothing die of all that is the children’s of Israel.
verses.bsb: But the LORD will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt, so that no animal belonging to the Israelites will die.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord appointed a set time, saying, To morrow the Lord shall do this thing in the land.
verses.bsb: The LORD set a time, saying, “Tomorrow the LORD will do this in the land.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord did that thing on the morrow, and all the cattle of Egypt died: but of the cattle of the children of Israel died not one.
verses.bsb: And the next day the LORD did just that. All the livestock of the Egyptians died, but not one animal belonging to the Israelites died.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh sent, and, behold, there was not one of the cattle of the Israelites dead. And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh sent officials and found that none of the livestock of the Israelites had died. But Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not let the people go.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses and unto Aaron, Take to you handfuls of ashes of the furnace, and let Moses sprinkle it toward the heaven in the sight of Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, “Take handfuls of soot from the furnace; in the sight of Pharaoh, Moses is to toss it into the air.

verses.kjv: And it shall become small dust in all the land of Egypt, and shall be a boil breaking forth with blains upon man, and upon beast, throughout all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: It will become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and festering boils will break out on man and beast throughout the land.”

verses.kjv: And they took ashes of the furnace, and stood before Pharaoh; and Moses sprinkled it up toward heaven; and it became a boil breaking forth with blains upon man, and upon beast.
verses.bsb: So they took soot from the furnace and stood before Pharaoh. Moses tossed it into the air, and festering boils broke out on man and beast.

verses.kjv: And the magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils; for the boil was upon the magicians, and upon all the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: The magicians could not stand before Moses, because the boils had broken out on them and on all the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he hearkened not unto them; as the Lord had spoken unto Moses.
verses.bsb: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not listen to them, just as the LORD had said to Moses.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh, and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord God of the Hebrews, Let my people go, that they may serve me.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Get up early in the morning, stand before Pharaoh, and tell him that this is what the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.

verses.kjv: For I will at this time send all my plagues upon thine heart, and upon thy servants, and upon thy people; that thou mayest know that there is none like me in all the earth.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, I will send all My plagues against you and your officials and your people, so you may know that there is no one like Me in all the earth.

verses.kjv: For now I will stretch out my hand, that I may smite thee and thy people with pestilence; and thou shalt be cut off from the earth.
verses.bsb: For by this time I could have stretched out My hand and struck you and your people with a plague to wipe you off the earth.

verses.kjv: And in very deed for this cause have I raised thee up, for to shew in thee my power; and that my name may be declared throughout all the earth.
verses.bsb: But I have raised you up for this very purpose, that I might display My power to you, and that My name might be proclaimed in all the earth.

verses.kjv: As yet exaltest thou thyself against my people, that thou wilt not let them go?
verses.bsb: Still, you lord it over My people and do not allow them to go.

verses.kjv: Behold, to morrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as hath not been in Egypt since the foundation thereof even until now.
verses.bsb: Behold, at this time tomorrow I will rain down the worst hail that has ever fallen on Egypt, from the day it was founded until now.

verses.kjv: Send therefore now, and gather thy cattle, and all that thou hast in the field; for upon every man and beast which shall be found in the field, and shall not be brought home, the hail shall come down upon them, and they shall die.
verses.bsb: So give orders now to shelter your livestock and everything you have in the field. Every man or beast that remains in the field and is not brought inside will die when the hail comes down upon them.’”

verses.kjv: He that feared the word of the Lord among the servants of Pharaoh made his servants and his cattle flee into the houses:
verses.bsb: Those among Pharaoh’s officials who feared the word of the LORD hurried to bring their servants and livestock to shelter,

verses.kjv: And he that regarded not the word of the Lord left his servants and his cattle in the field.
verses.bsb: but those who disregarded the word of the LORD left their servants and livestock in the field.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch forth thine hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, upon man, and upon beast, and upon every herb of the field, throughout the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward heaven, so that hail may fall on all the land of Egypt—on man and beast and every plant of the field throughout the land of Egypt.”

verses.kjv: And Moses stretched forth his rod toward heaven: and the Lord sent thunder and hail, and the fire ran along upon the ground; and the Lord rained hail upon the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: So Moses stretched out his staff toward heaven, and the LORD sent thunder and hail, and lightning struck the earth. So the LORD rained down hail upon the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: So there was hail, and fire mingled with the hail, very grievous, such as there was none like it in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.
verses.bsb: The hail fell and the lightning continued flashing through it. The hail was so severe that nothing like it had ever been seen in all the land of Egypt from the time it became a nation.

verses.kjv: And the hail smote throughout all the land of Egypt all that was in the field, both man and beast; and the hail smote every herb of the field, and brake every tree of the field.
verses.bsb: Throughout the land of Egypt, the hail struck down everything in the field, both man and beast; it beat down every plant of the field and stripped every tree.

verses.kjv: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were, was there no hail.
verses.bsb: The only place where it did not hail was in the land of Goshen, where the Israelites lived.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Pharaoh sent, and called for Moses and Aaron, and said unto them, I have sinned this time: the Lord is righteous, and I and my people are wicked.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron. “This time I have sinned,” he said. “The LORD is righteous, and I and my people are wicked.

verses.kjv: Intreat the Lord (for it is enough) that there be no more mighty thunderings and hail; and I will let you go, and ye shall stay no longer.
verses.bsb: Pray to the LORD, for there has been enough of God’s thunder and hail. I will let you go; you do not need to stay any longer.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto him, As soon as I am gone out of the city, I will spread abroad my hands unto the Lord; and the thunder shall cease, neither shall there be any more hail; that thou mayest know how that the earth is the Lord’s.
verses.bsb: Moses said to him, “When I have left the city, I will spread out my hands to the LORD. The thunder will cease, and there will be no more hail, so that you may know that the earth is the LORD’s.

verses.kjv: But as for thee and thy servants, I know that ye will not yet fear the Lord God.
verses.bsb: But as for you and your officials, I know that you still do not fear the LORD our God.”

verses.kjv: And the flax and the barley was smitten: for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was bolled.
verses.bsb: (Now the flax and barley were destroyed, since the barley was ripe and the flax was in bloom;

verses.kjv: But the wheat and the rie were not smitten: for they were not grown up.
verses.bsb: but the wheat and spelt were not destroyed, because they are late crops.)

verses.kjv: And Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh, and spread abroad his hands unto the Lord: and the thunders and hail ceased, and the rain was not poured upon the earth.
verses.bsb: Then Moses departed from Pharaoh, went out of the city, and spread out his hands to the LORD. The thunder and hail ceased, and the rain no longer poured down on the land.

verses.kjv: And when Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders were ceased, he sinned yet more, and hardened his heart, he and his servants.
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh saw that the rain and hail and thunder had ceased, he sinned again and hardened his heart—he and his officials.

verses.kjv: And the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, neither would he let the children of Israel go; as the Lord had spoken by Moses.
verses.bsb: So Pharaoh’s heart was hardened, and he would not let the Israelites go, just as the LORD had said through Moses.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, Go in unto Pharaoh: for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that I might shew these my signs before him:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart and the hearts of his officials, that I may perform these miraculous signs of Mine among them,

verses.kjv: And that thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son’s son, what things I have wrought in Egypt, and my signs which I have done among them; that ye may know how that I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: and that you may tell your children and grandchildren how severely I dealt with the Egyptians when I performed miraculous signs among them, so that all of you may know that I am the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron came in unto Pharaoh, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord God of the Hebrews, How long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before me? let my people go, that they may serve me.
verses.bsb: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and told him, “This is what the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, says: ‘How long will you refuse to humble yourself before Me? Let My people go, so that they may worship Me.

verses.kjv: Else, if thou refuse to let my people go, behold, to morrow will I bring the locusts into thy coast:
verses.bsb: But if you refuse to let My people go, I will bring locusts into your territory tomorrow.

verses.kjv: And they shall cover the face of the earth, that one cannot be able to see the earth: and they shall eat the residue of that which is escaped, which remaineth unto you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which groweth for you out of the field:
verses.bsb: They will cover the face of the land so that no one can see it. They will devour whatever is left after the hail and eat every tree that grows in your fields.

verses.kjv: And they shall fill thy houses, and the houses of all thy servants, and the houses of all the Egyptians; which neither thy fathers, nor thy fathers’ fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day. And he turned himself, and went out from Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: They will fill your houses and the houses of all your officials and every Egyptian—something neither your fathers nor your grandfathers have seen since the day they came into this land.’”  Then Moses turned and left Pharaoh’s presence.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh’s servants said unto him, How long shall this man be a snare unto us? let the men go, that they may serve the Lord their God: knowest thou not yet that Egypt is destroyed?
verses.bsb: Pharaoh’s officials asked him, “How long will this man be a snare to us? Let the people go, so that they may worship the LORD their God. Do you not yet realize that Egypt is in ruins?”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron were brought again unto Pharaoh: and he said unto them, Go, serve the Lord your God: but who are they that shall go?
verses.bsb: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh. “Go, worship the LORD your God,” he said. “But who exactly will be going?”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, We will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds will we go; for we must hold a feast unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: “We will go with our young and old,” Moses replied. “We will go with our sons and daughters, and with our flocks and herds, for we must hold a feast to the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Let the Lord be so with you, as I will let you go, and your little ones: look to it; for evil is before you.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh told them, “May the LORD be with you if I ever let you go with your little ones. Clearly you are bent on evil.

verses.kjv: Not so: go now ye that are men, and serve the Lord; for that ye did desire. And they were driven out from Pharaoh’s presence.
verses.bsb: No, only the men may go and worship the LORD, since that is what you have been requesting.” And Moses and Aaron were driven from Pharaoh’s presence.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up upon the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail hath left.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt, so that the locusts may swarm over it and devour every plant in the land—everything that the hail has left behind.”

verses.kjv: And Moses stretched forth his rod over the land of Egypt, and the Lord brought an east wind upon the land all that day, and all that night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.
verses.bsb: So Moses stretched out his staff over the land of Egypt, and throughout that day and night the LORD sent an east wind across the land. By morning the east wind had brought the locusts.

verses.kjv: And the locusts went up over all the land of Egypt, and rested in all the coasts of Egypt: very grievous were they; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them shall be such.
verses.bsb: The locusts swarmed across the land and settled over the entire territory of Egypt. Never before had there been so many locusts, and never again will there be.

verses.kjv: For they covered the face of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened; and they did eat every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left: and there remained not any green thing in the trees, or in the herbs of the field, through all the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: They covered the face of all the land until it was black, and they consumed all the plants on the ground and all the fruit on the trees that the hail had left behind. Nothing green was left on any tree or plant in all the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron in haste; and he said, I have sinned against the Lord your God, and against you.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh quickly summoned Moses and Aaron and said, “I have sinned against the LORD your God and against you.

verses.kjv: Now therefore forgive, I pray thee, my sin only this once, and intreat the Lord your God, that he may take away from me this death only.
verses.bsb: Now please forgive my sin once more and appeal to the LORD your God, that He may remove this death from me.”

verses.kjv: And he went out from Pharaoh, and intreated the Lord.
verses.bsb: So Moses left Pharaoh’s presence and appealed to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the Lord turned a mighty strong west wind, which took away the locusts, and cast them into the Red sea; there remained not one locust in all the coasts of Egypt.
verses.bsb: And the LORD changed the wind to a very strong west wind that carried off the locusts and blew them into the Red Sea. Not a single locust remained anywhere in Egypt.

verses.kjv: But the Lord hardened Pharaoh’s heart, so that he would not let the children of Israel go.
verses.bsb: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let the Israelites go.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand toward heaven, so that darkness may spread over the land of Egypt—a palpable darkness.”
