verses.kjv: And Moses stretched forth his hand toward heaven; and there was a thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days:
verses.bsb: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and total darkness covered all the land of Egypt for three days.

verses.kjv: They saw not one another, neither rose any from his place for three days: but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
verses.bsb: No one could see anyone else, and for three days no one left his place. Yet all the Israelites had light in their dwellings.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Pharaoh called unto Moses, and said, Go ye, serve the Lord; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed: let your little ones also go with you.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and said, “Go, worship the LORD. Even your little ones may go with you; only your flocks and herds must stay behind.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Thou must give us also sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the Lord our God.
verses.bsb: But Moses replied, “You must also provide us with sacrifices and burnt offerings to present to the LORD our God.

verses.kjv: Our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not an hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the Lord our God; and we know not with what we must serve the Lord, until we come thither.
verses.bsb: Even our livestock must go with us; not a hoof will be left behind, for we will need some of them to worship the LORD our God, and we will not know how we are to worship the LORD until we arrive.”

verses.kjv: ¶ But the Lord hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let them go.
verses.bsb: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he was unwilling to let them go.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh said unto him, Get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in that day thou seest my face thou shalt die.
verses.bsb: “Depart from me!” Pharaoh said to Moses. “Make sure you never see my face again, for on the day you see my face, you will die.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Thou hast spoken well, I will see thy face again no more.
verses.bsb: “As you say,” Moses replied, “I will never see your face again.”

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, Yet will I bring one plague more upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence: when he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “I will bring upon Pharaoh and Egypt one more plague. After that, he will allow you to leave this place. And when he lets you go, he will drive you out completely.

verses.kjv: Speak now in the ears of the people, and let every man borrow of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.
verses.bsb: Now announce to the people that men and women alike should ask their neighbors for articles of silver and gold.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.
verses.bsb: And the LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moreover, Moses himself was highly regarded in Egypt by Pharaoh’s officials and by the people.

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Thus saith the Lord, About midnight will I go out into the midst of Egypt:
verses.bsb: So Moses declared, “This is what the LORD says: ‘About midnight I will go throughout Egypt,

verses.kjv: And all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sitteth upon his throne, even unto the firstborn of the maidservant that is behind the mill; and all the firstborn of beasts.
verses.bsb: and every firstborn son in the land of Egypt will die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, to the firstborn of the servant girl behind the hand mill, as well as the firstborn of all the cattle.

verses.kjv: And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as there was none like it, nor shall be like it any more.
verses.bsb: Then a great cry will go out over all the land of Egypt. Such an outcry has never been heard before and will never be heard again.

verses.kjv: But against any of the children of Israel shall not a dog move his tongue, against man or beast: that ye may know how that the Lord doth put a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.
verses.bsb: But among all the Israelites, not even a dog will snarl at man or beast.’  Then you will know that the LORD makes a distinction between Egypt and Israel.

verses.kjv: And all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down themselves unto me, saying, Get thee out, and all the people that follow thee: and after that I will go out. And he went out from Pharaoh in a great anger.
verses.bsb: And all these officials of yours will come and bow before me, saying, ‘Go, you and all the people who follow you!’ After that, I will depart.”  And hot with anger, Moses left Pharaoh’s presence.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Pharaoh shall not hearken unto you; that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: The LORD said to Moses, “Pharaoh will not listen to you, so that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh: and the Lord hardened Pharaoh’s heart, so that he would not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
verses.bsb: Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh, but the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart so that he would not let the Israelites go out of his land.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,
verses.bsb: Now the LORD said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt,

verses.kjv: This month shall be unto you the beginning of months: it shall be the first month of the year to you.
verses.bsb: “This month is the beginning of months for you; it shall be the first month of your year.

verses.kjv: ¶ Speak ye unto all the congregation of Israel, saying, In the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to the house of their fathers, a lamb for an house:
verses.bsb: Tell the whole congregation of Israel that on the tenth day of this month each man must select a lamb for his family, one per household.

verses.kjv: And if the household be too little for the lamb, let him and his neighbour next unto his house take it according to the number of the souls; every man according to his eating shall make your count for the lamb.
verses.bsb: If the household is too small for a whole lamb, they are to share with the nearest neighbor based on the number of people, and apportion the lamb accordingly.

verses.kjv: Your lamb shall be without blemish, a male of the first year: ye shall take it out from the sheep, or from the goats:
verses.bsb: Your lamb must be an unblemished year-old male, and you may take it from the sheep or the goats.

verses.kjv: And ye shall keep it up until the fourteenth day of the same month: and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it in the evening.
verses.bsb: You must keep it until the fourteenth day of the month, when the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel will slaughter the animals at twilight.

verses.kjv: And they shall take of the blood, and strike it on the two side posts and on the upper door post of the houses, wherein they shall eat it.
verses.bsb: They are to take some of the blood and put it on the sides and tops of the doorframes of the houses where they eat the lambs.

verses.kjv: And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; and with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
verses.bsb: They are to eat the meat that night, roasted over the fire, along with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.

verses.kjv: Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire; his head with his legs, and with the purtenance thereof.
verses.bsb: Do not eat any of the meat raw or cooked in boiling water, but only roasted over the fire—its head and legs and inner parts.

verses.kjv: And ye shall let nothing of it remain until the morning; and that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
verses.bsb: Do not leave any of it until morning; before the morning you must burn up any part that is left over.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thus shall ye eat it; with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste: it is the Lord’s passover.
verses.bsb: This is how you are to eat it: You must be fully dressed for travel, with your sandals on your feet and your staff in your hand. You are to eat in haste; it is the LORD’s Passover.

verses.kjv: For I will pass through the land of Egypt this night, and will smite all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: On that night I will pass through the land of Egypt and strike down every firstborn male, both man and beast, and I will execute judgment against all the gods of Egypt. I am the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are: and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and the plague shall not be upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: The blood on the houses where you are staying will distinguish them; when I see the blood, I will pass over you. No plague will fall on you to destroy you when I strike the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And this day shall be unto you for a memorial; and ye shall keep it a feast to the Lord throughout your generations; ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.
verses.bsb: And this day will be a memorial for you, and you are to celebrate it as a feast to the LORD, as a permanent statute for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; even the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses: for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.
verses.bsb: For seven days you must eat unleavened bread. On the first day you are to remove the leaven from your houses. Whoever eats anything leavened from the first day through the seventh must be cut off from Israel.

verses.kjv: And in the first day there shall be an holy convocation, and in the seventh day there shall be an holy convocation to you; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done of you.
verses.bsb: On the first day you are to hold a sacred assembly, and another on the seventh day. You must not do any work on those days, except to prepare the meals—that is all you may do.

verses.kjv: And ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have I brought your armies out of the land of Egypt: therefore shall ye observe this day in your generations by an ordinance for ever.
verses.bsb: So you are to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread, for on this very day I brought your divisions out of the land of Egypt. You must keep this day as a permanent statute for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: ¶ In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.
verses.bsb: In the first month you are to eat unleavened bread, from the evening of the fourteenth day until the evening of the twenty-first day.

verses.kjv: Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses: for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, even that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he be a stranger, or born in the land.
verses.bsb: For seven days there must be no leaven found in your houses. If anyone eats something leavened, that person, whether a foreigner or native of the land, must be cut off from the congregation of Israel.

verses.kjv: Ye shall eat nothing leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.
verses.bsb: You are not to eat anything leavened; eat unleavened bread in all your homes.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel, and said unto them, Draw out and take you a lamb according to your families, and kill the passover.
verses.bsb: Then Moses summoned all the elders of Israel and told them, “Go at once and select for yourselves a lamb for each family, and slaughter the Passover lamb.

verses.kjv: And ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the bason, and strike the lintel and the two side posts with the blood that is in the bason; and none of you shall go out at the door of his house until the morning.
verses.bsb: Take a cluster of hyssop, dip it into the blood in the basin, and brush the blood on the top and sides of the doorframe. None of you shall go out the door of his house until morning.

verses.kjv: For the Lord will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when he seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side posts, the Lord will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.
verses.bsb: When the LORD passes through to strike down the Egyptians, He will see the blood on the top and sides of the doorframe and will pass over that doorway; so He will not allow the destroyer to enter your houses and strike you down.

verses.kjv: And ye shall observe this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.
verses.bsb: And you are to keep this command as a permanent statute for you and your descendants.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the Lord will give you, according as he hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.
verses.bsb: When you enter the land that the LORD will give you as He promised, you are to keep this service.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you, What mean ye by this service?
verses.bsb: When your children ask you, ‘What does this service mean to you?’

verses.kjv: That ye shall say, It is the sacrifice of the Lord’s passover, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when he smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses. And the people bowed the head and worshipped.
verses.bsb: you are to reply, ‘It is the Passover sacrifice to the LORD, who passed over the houses of the Israelites in Egypt when He struck down the Egyptians and spared our homes.’”  Then the people bowed down and worshiped.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel went away, and did as the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
verses.bsb: And the Israelites went and did just what the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that at midnight the Lord smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle.
verses.bsb: Now at midnight the LORD struck down every firstborn male in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh, who sat on his throne, to the firstborn of the prisoner in the dungeon, as well as all the firstborn among the livestock.

verses.kjv: And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
verses.bsb: During the night Pharaoh got up—he and all his officials and all the Egyptians—and there was loud wailing in Egypt; for there was no house without someone dead.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he called for Moses and Aaron by night, and said, Rise up, and get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the Lord, as ye have said.
verses.bsb: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron by night and said, “Get up, leave my people, both you and the Israelites! Go, worship the LORD as you have requested.

verses.kjv: Also take your flocks and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.
verses.bsb: Take your flocks and herds as well, just as you have said, and depart! And bless me also.”

verses.kjv: And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, that they might send them out of the land in haste; for they said, We be all dead men.
verses.bsb: And in order to send them out of the land quickly, the Egyptians urged the people on. “For otherwise,” they said, “we are all going to die!”

verses.kjv: And the people took their dough before it was leavened, their kneadingtroughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
verses.bsb: So the people took their dough before it was leavened, carrying it on their shoulders in kneading bowls wrapped in clothing.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel did according to the word of Moses; and they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment:
verses.bsb: Furthermore, the Israelites acted on Moses’ word and asked the Egyptians for articles of silver and gold, and for clothing.

verses.kjv: And the Lord gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they lent unto them such things as they required. And they spoiled the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: And the LORD gave the people such favor in the sight of the Egyptians that they granted their request. In this way they plundered the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand on foot that were men, beside children.
verses.bsb: The Israelites journeyed from Rameses to Succoth with about 600,000 men on foot, besides women and children.

verses.kjv: And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.
verses.bsb: And a mixed multitude also went up with them, along with great droves of livestock, both flocks and herds.

verses.kjv: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.
verses.bsb: Since their dough had no leaven, the people baked what they had brought out of Egypt into unleavened loaves. For when they had been driven out of Egypt, they could not delay and had not prepared any provisions for themselves.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now the sojourning of the children of Israel, who dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years.
verses.bsb: Now the duration of the Israelites’ stay in Egypt was 430 years.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the hosts of the Lord went out from the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: At the end of the 430 years, to the very day, all the LORD’s divisions went out of the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: It is a night to be much observed unto the Lord for bringing them out from the land of Egypt: this is that night of the Lord to be observed of all the children of Israel in their generations.
verses.bsb: Because the LORD kept a vigil that night to bring them out of the land of Egypt, this same night is to be a vigil to the LORD, to be observed by all the Israelites for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses and Aaron, This is the ordinance of the passover: There shall no stranger eat thereof:
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, “This is the statute of the Passover: No foreigner is to eat of it.

verses.kjv: But every man’s servant that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
verses.bsb: But any slave who has been purchased may eat of it, after you have circumcised him.

verses.kjv: A foreigner and an hired servant shall not eat thereof.
verses.bsb: A temporary resident or hired hand shall not eat the Passover.

verses.kjv: In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth ought of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
verses.bsb: It must be eaten inside one house. You are not to take any of the meat outside the house, and you may not break any of the bones.

verses.kjv: All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
verses.bsb: The whole congregation of Israel must celebrate it.

verses.kjv: And when a stranger shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the Lord, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land: for no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
verses.bsb: If a foreigner resides with you and wants to celebrate the LORD’s Passover, all the males in the household must be circumcised; then he may come near to celebrate it, and he shall be like a native of the land. But no uncircumcised man may eat of it.

verses.kjv: One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.
verses.bsb: The same law shall apply to both the native and the foreigner who resides among you.”

verses.kjv: Thus did all the children of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
verses.bsb: Then all the Israelites did this—they did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass the selfsame day, that the Lord did bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt by their armies.
verses.bsb: And on that very day the LORD brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt by their divisions.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Sanctify unto me all the firstborn, whatsoever openeth the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast: it is mine.
verses.bsb: “Consecrate to Me every firstborn male. The firstborn from every womb among the Israelites belongs to Me, both of man and beast.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the people, Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the Lord brought you out from this place: there shall no leavened bread be eaten.
verses.bsb: So Moses told the people, “Remember this day, the day you came out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery; for the LORD brought you out of it by the strength of His hand. And nothing leavened shall be eaten.

verses.kjv: This day came ye out in the month Abib.
verses.bsb: Today, in the month of Abib, you are leaving.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall be when the Lord shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, and the Hittites, and the Amorites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, which he sware unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.
verses.bsb: And when the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Hivites, and Jebusites—the land He swore to your fathers that He would give you, a land flowing with milk and honey—you shall keep this service in this month.

verses.kjv: Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the Lord.
verses.bsb: For seven days you are to eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a feast to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Unleavened bread shall be eaten seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee in all thy quarters.
verses.bsb: Unleavened bread shall be eaten during those seven days. Nothing leavened may be found among you, nor shall leaven be found anywhere within your borders.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt shew thy son in that day, saying, This is done because of that which the Lord did unto me when I came forth out of Egypt.
verses.bsb: And on that day you are to explain to your son, ‘This is because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.’

verses.kjv: And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thine hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the Lord’s law may be in thy mouth: for with a strong hand hath the Lord brought thee out of Egypt.
verses.bsb: It shall be a sign for you on your hand and a reminder on your forehead that the Law of the LORD is to be on your lips. For with a mighty hand the LORD brought you out of Egypt.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt therefore keep this ordinance in his season from year to year.
verses.bsb: Therefore you shall keep this statute at the appointed time year after year.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall be when the Lord shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanites, as he sware unto thee and to thy fathers, and shall give it thee,
verses.bsb: And after the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites and gives it to you, as He swore to you and your fathers,

verses.kjv: That thou shalt set apart unto the Lord all that openeth the matrix, and every firstling that cometh of a beast which thou hast; the males shall be the Lord’s.
verses.bsb: you are to present to the LORD the firstborn male of every womb. All the firstborn males of your livestock belong to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And every firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break his neck: and all the firstborn of man among thy children shalt thou redeem.
verses.bsb: You must redeem every firstborn donkey with a lamb, and if you do not redeem it, you are to break its neck. And every firstborn of your sons you must redeem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall be when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What is this? that thou shalt say unto him, By strength of hand the Lord brought us out from Egypt, from the house of bondage:
verses.bsb: In the future, when your son asks you, ‘What does this mean?’ you are to tell him, ‘With a mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh would hardly let us go, that the Lord slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man, and the firstborn of beast: therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all that openeth the matrix, being males; but all the firstborn of my children I redeem.
verses.bsb: And when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the LORD killed every firstborn in the land of Egypt, both of man and beast. This is why I sacrifice to the LORD the firstborn male of every womb, but I redeem all the firstborn of my sons.’

verses.kjv: And it shall be for a token upon thine hand, and for frontlets between thine eyes: for by strength of hand the Lord brought us forth out of Egypt.
verses.bsb: So it shall serve as a sign on your hand and a symbol on your forehead, for with a mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God led them not through the way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near; for God said, Lest peradventure the people repent when they see war, and they return to Egypt:
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them along the road through the land of the Philistines, though it was shorter. For God said, “If the people face war, they might change their minds and return to Egypt.”

verses.kjv: But God led the people about, through the way of the wilderness of the Red sea: and the children of Israel went up harnessed out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: So God led the people around by the way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea. And the Israelites left the land of Egypt arrayed for battle.

verses.kjv: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him: for he had straitly sworn the children of Israel, saying, God will surely visit you; and ye shall carry up my bones away hence with you.
verses.bsb: Moses took the bones of Joseph with him because Joseph had made the sons of Israel swear a solemn oath when he said, “God will surely attend to you, and then you must carry my bones with you from this place.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they took their journey from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, in the edge of the wilderness.
verses.bsb: They set out from Succoth and camped at Etham on the edge of the wilderness.

verses.kjv: And the Lord went before them by day in a pillar of a cloud, to lead them the way; and by night in a pillar of fire, to give them light; to go by day and night:
verses.bsb: And the LORD went before them in a pillar of cloud to guide their way by day, and in a pillar of fire to give them light by night, so that they could travel by day or night.

verses.kjv: He took not away the pillar of the cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, from before the people.
verses.bsb: Neither the pillar of cloud by day nor the pillar of fire by night left its place before the people.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they turn and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, over against Baal-zephon: before it shall ye encamp by the sea.
verses.bsb: “Tell the Israelites to turn back and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea. You are to encamp by the sea, directly opposite Baal-zephon.

verses.kjv: For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, They are entangled in the land, the wilderness hath shut them in.
verses.bsb: For Pharaoh will say of the Israelites, ‘They are wandering the land in confusion; the wilderness has boxed them in.’

verses.kjv: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, that he shall follow after them; and I will be honoured upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host; that the Egyptians may know that I am the Lord. And they did so.
verses.bsb: And I will harden Pharaoh’s heart so that he will pursue them. But I will gain honor by means of Pharaoh and all his army, and the Egyptians will know that I am the LORD.”  So this is what the Israelites did.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it was told the king of Egypt that the people fled: and the heart of Pharaoh and of his servants was turned against the people, and they said, Why have we done this, that we have let Israel go from serving us?
verses.bsb: When the king of Egypt was told that the people had fled, Pharaoh and his officials changed their minds about them and said, “What have we done? We have released Israel from serving us.”

verses.kjv: And he made ready his chariot, and took his people with him:
verses.bsb: So Pharaoh prepared his chariot and took his army with him.

verses.kjv: And he took six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over every one of them.
verses.bsb: He took 600 of the best chariots, and all the other chariots of Egypt, with officers over all of them.

verses.kjv: And the Lord hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel: and the children of Israel went out with an high hand.
verses.bsb: And the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt so that he pursued the Israelites, who were marching out defiantly.

verses.kjv: But the Egyptians pursued after them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, and his horsemen, and his army, and overtook them encamping by the sea, beside Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.
verses.bsb: The Egyptians—all Pharaoh’s horses and chariots, horsemen and troops—pursued the Israelites and overtook them as they camped by the sea near Pi-hahiroth, opposite Baal-zephon.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Pharaoh drew nigh, the children of Israel lifted up their eyes, and, behold, the Egyptians marched after them; and they were sore afraid: and the children of Israel cried out unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: As Pharaoh approached, the Israelites looked up and saw the Egyptians marching after them, and they were terrified and cried out to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And they said unto Moses, Because there were no graves in Egypt, hast thou taken us away to die in the wilderness? wherefore hast thou dealt thus with us, to carry us forth out of Egypt?
verses.bsb: They said to Moses, “Was it because there were no graves in Egypt that you brought us into the wilderness to die? What have you done to us by bringing us out of Egypt?

verses.kjv: Is not this the word that we did tell thee in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, that we may serve the Egyptians? For it had been better for us to serve the Egyptians, than that we should die in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: Did we not say to you in Egypt, ‘Leave us alone so that we may serve the Egyptians’? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the people, Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will shew to you to day: for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever.
verses.bsb: But Moses told the people, “Do not be afraid. Stand firm and you will see the LORD’s salvation, which He will accomplish for you today; for the Egyptians you see today, you will never see again.

verses.kjv: The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
verses.bsb: The LORD will fight for you; you need only to be still.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel, that they go forward:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Why are you crying out to Me? Tell the Israelites to go forward.

verses.kjv: But lift thou up thy rod, and stretch out thine hand over the sea, and divide it: and the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea.
verses.bsb: And as for you, lift up your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, so that the Israelites can go through the sea on dry ground.

verses.kjv: And I, behold, I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians, and they shall follow them: and I will get me honour upon Pharaoh, and upon all his host, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
verses.bsb: And I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they will go in after them. Then I will gain honor by means of Pharaoh and all his army and chariots and horsemen.

verses.kjv: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the Lord, when I have gotten me honour upon Pharaoh, upon his chariots, and upon his horsemen.
verses.bsb: The Egyptians will know that I am the LORD when I am honored through Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the angel of God, which went before the camp of Israel, removed and went behind them; and the pillar of the cloud went from before their face, and stood behind them:
verses.bsb: And the angel of God, who had gone before the camp of Israel, withdrew and went behind them. The pillar of cloud also moved from before them and stood behind them,

verses.kjv: And it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel; and it was a cloud and darkness to them, but it gave light by night to these: so that the one came not near the other all the night.
verses.bsb: so that it came between the camps of Egypt and Israel. The cloud was there in the darkness, but it lit up the night. So all night long neither camp went near the other.

verses.kjv: And Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night, and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.
verses.bsb: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and all that night the LORD drove back the sea with a strong east wind that turned it into dry land. So the waters were divided,

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground: and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
verses.bsb: and the Israelites went through the sea on dry ground, with walls of water on their right and on their left.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Egyptians pursued, and went in after them to the midst of the sea, even all Pharaoh’s horses, his chariots, and his horsemen.
verses.bsb: And the Egyptians chased after them—all Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, and horsemen—and followed them into the sea.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that in the morning watch the Lord looked unto the host of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and of the cloud, and troubled the host of the Egyptians,
verses.bsb: At morning watch, however, the LORD looked down on the army of the Egyptians from the pillar of fire and cloud, and He threw their camp into confusion.

verses.kjv: And took off their chariot wheels, that they drave them heavily: so that the Egyptians said, Let us flee from the face of Israel; for the Lord fighteth for them against the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: He caused their chariot wheels to wobble, so that they had difficulty driving. “Let us flee from the Israelites,” said the Egyptians, “for the LORD is fighting for them against Egypt!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Stretch out thine hand over the sea, that the waters may come again upon the Egyptians, upon their chariots, and upon their horsemen.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Stretch out your hand over the sea, so that the waters may flow back over the Egyptians and their chariots and horsemen.”

verses.kjv: And Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to his strength when the morning appeared; and the Egyptians fled against it; and the Lord overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
verses.bsb: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and at daybreak the sea returned to its normal state. As the Egyptians were retreating, the LORD swept them into the sea.

verses.kjv: And the waters returned, and covered the chariots, and the horsemen, and all the host of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them; there remained not so much as one of them.
verses.bsb: The waters flowed back and covered the chariots and horsemen—the entire army of Pharaoh that had chased the Israelites into the sea. Not one of them survived.

verses.kjv: But the children of Israel walked upon dry land in the midst of the sea; and the waters were a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.
verses.bsb: But the Israelites had walked through the sea on dry ground, with walls of water on their right and on their left.

verses.kjv: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore.
verses.bsb: That day the LORD saved Israel from the hand of the Egyptians, and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the shore.

verses.kjv: And Israel saw that great work which the Lord did upon the Egyptians: and the people feared the Lord, and believed the Lord, and his servant Moses.
verses.bsb: When Israel saw the great power that the LORD had exercised over the Egyptians, the people feared the LORD and believed in Him and in His servant Moses.

verses.kjv: THEN sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto the Lord, and spake, saying, I will sing unto the Lord, for he hath triumphed gloriously: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
verses.bsb: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the LORD:  “I will sing to the LORD, for He is highly exalted. The horse and rider He has thrown into the sea.

verses.kjv: The Lord is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father’s God, and I will exalt him.
verses.bsb: The LORD is my strength and my song, and He has become my salvation. He is my God, and I will praise Him, my father’s God, and I will exalt Him.

verses.kjv: The Lord is a man of war: the Lord is his name.
verses.bsb: The LORD is a warrior, the LORD is His name.

verses.kjv: Pharaoh’s chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea: his chosen captains also are drowned in the Red sea.
verses.bsb: Pharaoh’s chariots and army He has cast into the sea; the finest of his officers are drowned in the Red Sea.

verses.kjv: The depths have covered them: they sank into the bottom as a stone.
verses.bsb: The depths have covered them; they sank there like a stone.

verses.kjv: Thy right hand, O Lord, is become glorious in power: thy right hand, O Lord, hath dashed in pieces the enemy.
verses.bsb: Your right hand, O LORD, is majestic in power; Your right hand, O LORD, has shattered the enemy.

verses.kjv: And in the greatness of thine excellency thou hast overthrown them that rose up against thee: thou sentest forth thy wrath, which consumed them as stubble.
verses.bsb: You overthrew Your adversaries by Your great majesty. You unleashed Your burning wrath; it consumed them like stubble.

verses.kjv: And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were gathered together, the floods stood upright as an heap, and the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.
verses.bsb: At the blast of Your nostrils the waters piled up; like a wall the currents stood firm; the depths congealed in the heart of the sea.

verses.kjv: The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.
verses.bsb: The enemy declared, ‘I will pursue, I will overtake. I will divide the spoils; I will gorge myself on them. I will draw my sword; my hand will destroy them.’

verses.kjv: Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them: they sank as lead in the mighty waters.
verses.bsb: But You blew with Your breath, and the sea covered them. They sank like lead in the mighty waters.

verses.kjv: Who is like unto thee, O Lord, among the gods? who is like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?
verses.bsb: Who among the gods is like You, O LORD? Who is like You—majestic in holiness, revered with praises, performing wonders?

verses.kjv: Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, the earth swallowed them.
verses.bsb: You stretched out Your right hand, and the earth swallowed them up.

verses.kjv: Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people which thou hast redeemed: thou hast guided them in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.
verses.bsb: With loving devotion You will lead the people You have redeemed; with Your strength You will guide them to Your holy dwelling.

verses.kjv: The people shall hear, and be afraid: sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of Palestina.
verses.bsb: The nations will hear and tremble; anguish will grip the dwellers of Philistia.

verses.kjv: Then the dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab, trembling shall take hold upon them; all the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away.
verses.bsb: Then the chiefs of Edom will be dismayed; trembling will seize the leaders of Moab; those who dwell in Canaan will melt away,

verses.kjv: Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of thine arm they shall be as still as a stone; till thy people pass over, O Lord, till the people pass over, which thou hast purchased.
verses.bsb: and terror and dread will fall on them. By the power of Your arm they will be as still as a stone until Your people pass by, O LORD, until the people You have bought pass by.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, in the place, O Lord, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, in the Sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.
verses.bsb: You will bring them in and plant them on the mountain of Your inheritance— the place, O LORD, You have prepared for Your dwelling, the sanctuary, O Lord, Your hands have established.

verses.kjv: The Lord shall reign for ever and ever.
verses.bsb: The LORD will reign forever and ever!”

verses.kjv: For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the Lord brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land in the midst of the sea.
verses.bsb: For when Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, and horsemen went into the sea, the LORD brought the waters of the sea back over them. But the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
verses.bsb: Then Miriam the prophetess, Aaron’s sister, took a tambourine in her hand, and all the women followed her with tambourines and dancing.

verses.kjv: And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to the Lord, for he hath triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
verses.bsb: And Miriam sang back to them:  “Sing to the LORD, for He is highly exalted; the horse and rider He has thrown into the sea.”

verses.kjv: So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.
verses.bsb: Then Moses led Israel from the Red Sea, and they went out into the Desert of Shur. For three days they walked in the desert without finding water.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.
verses.bsb: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink the water there because it was bitter. (That is why it was named Marah.)

verses.kjv: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?
verses.bsb: So the people grumbled against Moses, saying, “What are we to drink?”

verses.kjv: And he cried unto the Lord; and the Lord shewed him a tree, which when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet: there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them,
verses.bsb: And Moses cried out to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a log. And when he cast it into the waters, they were sweetened.  There the LORD made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there He tested them,

verses.kjv: And said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the Lord thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon the Egyptians: for I am the Lord that healeth thee.
verses.bsb: saying, “If you will listen carefully to the voice of the LORD your God, and do what is right in His eyes, and pay attention to His commands, and keep all His statutes, then I will not bring on you any of the diseases I inflicted on the Egyptians. For I am the LORD who heals you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they came to Elim, where were twelve wells of water, and threescore and ten palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters.
verses.bsb: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there by the waters.

verses.kjv: AND they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: On the fifteenth day of the second month after they had left the land of Egypt, the whole congregation of Israel set out from Elim and came to the Desert of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai.

verses.kjv: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:
verses.bsb: And there in the desert they all grumbled against Moses and Aaron.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel said unto them, Would to God we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh pots, and when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.
verses.bsb: “If only we had died by the LORD’s hand in the land of Egypt!” they said. “There we sat by pots of meat and ate our fill of bread, but you have brought us into this desert to starve this whole assembly to death!”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then said the Lord unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Behold, I will rain down bread from heaven for you. Each day the people are to go out and gather enough for that day. In this way I will test whether or not they will follow My instructions.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that on the sixth day they shall prepare that which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.
verses.bsb: Then on the sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it will be twice as much as they gather on the other days.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the Lord hath brought you out from the land of Egypt:
verses.bsb: So Moses and Aaron said to all the Israelites, “This evening you will know that it was the LORD who brought you out of the land of Egypt,

verses.kjv: And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the Lord; for that he heareth your murmurings against the Lord: and what are we, that ye murmur against us?
verses.bsb: and in the morning you will see the LORD’s glory, because He has heard your grumbling against Him. For who are we that you should grumble against us?”

verses.kjv: And Moses said, This shall be, when the Lord shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the Lord heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him: and what are we? your murmurings are not against us, but against the Lord.
verses.bsb: And Moses added, “The LORD will give you meat to eat this evening and bread to fill you in the morning, for He has heard your grumbling against Him. Who are we? Your grumblings are not against us but against the LORD.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses spake unto Aaron, Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, Come near before the Lord: for he hath heard your murmurings.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Tell the whole congregation of Israel, ‘Come before the LORD, for He has heard your grumbling.’”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as Aaron spake unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the Lord appeared in the cloud.
verses.bsb: And as Aaron was speaking to the whole congregation of Israel, they looked toward the desert, and there in a cloud the glory of the LORD appeared.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak unto them, saying, At even ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: “I have heard the grumbling of the Israelites. Tell them, ‘At twilight you will eat meat, and in the morning you will be filled with bread. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God.’”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that at even the quails came up, and covered the camp: and in the morning the dew lay round about the host.
verses.bsb: That evening quail came and covered the camp, and in the morning there was a layer of dew around the camp.

verses.kjv: And when the dew that lay was gone up, behold, upon the face of the wilderness there lay a small round thing, as small as the hoar frost on the ground.
verses.bsb: When the layer of dew had evaporated, there were thin flakes on the desert floor, as fine as frost on the ground.

verses.kjv: And when the children of Israel saw it, they said one to another, It is manna: for they wist not what it was. And Moses said unto them, This is the bread which the Lord hath given you to eat.
verses.bsb: When the Israelites saw it, they asked one another, “What is it?” For they did not know what it was.  So Moses told them, “It is the bread that the LORD has given you to eat.

verses.kjv: ¶ This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded, Gather of it every man according to his eating, an omer for every man, according to the number of your persons; take ye every man for them which are in his tents.
verses.bsb: This is what the LORD has commanded: ‘Each one is to gather as much as he needs. You may take an omer for each person in your tent.’”

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some more, some less.
verses.bsb: So the Israelites did this. Some gathered more, and some less.

verses.kjv: And when they did mete it with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.
verses.bsb: When they measured it by the omer, he who gathered much had no excess, and he who gathered little had no shortfall. Each one gathered as much as he needed to eat.

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Let no man leave of it till the morning.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to them, “No one may keep any of it until morning.”

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and stank: and Moses was wroth with them.
verses.bsb: But they did not listen to Moses; some people left part of it until morning, and it became infested with maggots and began to smell. So Moses was angry with them.

verses.kjv: And they gathered it every morning, every man according to his eating: and when the sun waxed hot, it melted.
verses.bsb: Every morning each one gathered as much as was needed, and when the sun grew hot, it melted away.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one man: and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.
verses.bsb: On the sixth day, they gathered twice as much food—two omers per person—and all the leaders of the congregation came and reported this to Moses.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, This is that which the Lord hath said, To morrow is the rest of the holy sabbath unto the Lord: bake that which ye will bake to day, and seethe that ye will seethe; and that which remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.
verses.bsb: He told them, “This is what the LORD has said: ‘Tomorrow is to be a day of complete rest, a holy Sabbath to the LORD. So bake what you want to bake, and boil what you want to boil. Then set aside whatever remains and keep it until morning.’”

verses.kjv: And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.
verses.bsb: So they set it aside until morning as Moses had commanded, and it did not smell or contain any maggots.

verses.kjv: And Moses said, Eat that to day; for to day is a sabbath unto the Lord: to day ye shall not find it in the field.
verses.bsb: “Eat it today,” Moses said, “because today is a Sabbath to the LORD. Today you will not find anything in the field.

verses.kjv: Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day, which is the sabbath, in it there shall be none.
verses.bsb: For six days you may gather, but on the seventh day, the Sabbath, it will not be there.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that there went out some of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.
verses.bsb: Yet on the seventh day some of the people went out to gather, but they did not find anything.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “How long will you refuse to keep My commandments and instructions?

verses.kjv: See, for that the Lord hath given you the sabbath, therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.
verses.bsb: Understand that the LORD has given you the Sabbath; that is why on the sixth day He will give you bread for two days. On the seventh day, everyone must stay where he is; no one may leave his place.”

verses.kjv: So the people rested on the seventh day.
verses.bsb: So the people rested on the seventh day.

verses.kjv: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it was like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
verses.bsb: Now the house of Israel called the bread manna. It was white like coriander seed and tasted like wafers made with honey.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said, This is the thing which the Lord commandeth, Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I have fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Moses said, “This is what the LORD has commanded: ‘Keep an omer of manna for the generations to come, so that they may see the bread I fed you in the wilderness when I brought you out of the land of Egypt.’”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a pot, and put an omer full of manna therein, and lay it up before the Lord, to be kept for your generations.
verses.bsb: So Moses told Aaron, “Take a jar and fill it with an omer of manna. Then place it before the LORD to be preserved for the generations to come.”

verses.kjv: As the Lord commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.
verses.bsb: And Aaron placed it in front of the Testimony, to be preserved just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel did eat manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: The Israelites ate manna forty years, until they came to a land where they could settle; they ate manna until they reached the border of Canaan.

verses.kjv: Now an omer is the tenth part of an ephah.
verses.bsb: (Now an omer is a tenth of an ephah.)

verses.kjv: AND all the congregation of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin, after their journeys, according to the commandment of the Lord, and pitched in Rephidim: and there was no water for the people to drink.
verses.bsb: Then the whole congregation of Israel left the Desert of Sin, moving from place to place as the LORD commanded. They camped at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.

verses.kjv: Wherefore the people did chide with Moses, and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said unto them, Why chide ye with me? wherefore do ye tempt the Lord?
verses.bsb: So the people contended with Moses, “Give us water to drink.”  “Why do you contend with me?” Moses replied. “Why do you test the LORD?”

verses.kjv: And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, Wherefore is this that thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?
verses.bsb: But the people thirsted for water there, and they grumbled against Moses: “Why have you brought us out of Egypt—to make us and our children and livestock die of thirst?”

verses.kjv: And Moses cried unto the Lord, saying, What shall I do unto this people? they be almost ready to stone me.
verses.bsb: Then Moses cried out to the LORD, “What should I do with these people? A little more and they will stone me!”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thine hand, and go.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses, “Walk on ahead of the people and take some of the elders of Israel with you. Take along in your hand the staff with which you struck the Nile, and go.

verses.kjv: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.
verses.bsb: Behold, I will stand there before you by the rock at Horeb. And when you strike the rock, water will come out of it for the people to drink.”  So Moses did this in the sight of the elders of Israel.

verses.kjv: And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the Lord, saying, Is the Lord among us, or not?
verses.bsb: He named the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarreled, and because they tested the LORD, saying, “Is the LORD among us or not?”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel in Rephidim.
verses.bsb: After this, the Amalekites came and attacked the Israelites at Rephidim.

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto Joshua, Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek: to morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in mine hand.
verses.bsb: So Moses said to Joshua, “Choose some of our men and go out to fight the Amalekites. Tomorrow I will stand on the hilltop with the staff of God in my hand.”

verses.kjv: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, and fought with Amalek: and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
verses.bsb: Joshua did as Moses had instructed him and fought against the Amalekites, while Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed: and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.
verses.bsb: As long as Moses held up his hands, Israel prevailed; but when he lowered them, Amalek prevailed.

verses.kjv: But Moses’ hands were heavy; and they took a stone, and put it under him, and he sat thereon; and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.
verses.bsb: When Moses’ hands grew heavy, they took a stone and put it under him, and he sat on it. Then Aaron and Hur held his hands up, one on each side, so that his hands remained steady until the sun went down.

verses.kjv: And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.
verses.bsb: So Joshua overwhelmed Amalek and his army with the sword.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua: for I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Write this on a scroll as a reminder and recite it to Joshua, because I will utterly blot out the memory of Amalek from under heaven.”

verses.kjv: And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Jehovah-nissi:
verses.bsb: And Moses built an altar and named it The LORD Is My Banner.

verses.kjv: For he said, Because the Lord hath sworn that the Lord will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.
verses.bsb: “Indeed,” he said, “a hand was lifted up toward the throne of the LORD. The LORD will war against Amalek from generation to generation.”

verses.kjv: WHEN Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses’ father in law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, and that the Lord had brought Israel out of Egypt;
verses.bsb: Now Moses’ father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian, heard about all that God had done for Moses and His people Israel, and how the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.

verses.kjv: Then Jethro, Moses’ father in law, took Zipporah, Moses’ wife, after he had sent her back,
verses.bsb: After Moses had sent back his wife Zipporah, his father-in-law Jethro had received her,

verses.kjv: And her two sons; of which the name of the one was Gershom; for he said, I have been an alien in a strange land:
verses.bsb: along with her two sons. One son was named Gershom, for Moses had said, “I have been a foreigner in a foreign land.”

verses.kjv: And the name of the other was Eliezer; for the God of my father, said he, was mine help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh:
verses.bsb: The other son was named Eliezer, for Moses had said, “The God of my father was my helper and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.”

verses.kjv: And Jethro, Moses’ father in law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness, where he encamped at the mount of God:
verses.bsb: Moses’ father-in-law Jethro, along with Moses’ wife and sons, came to him in the desert, where he was encamped at the mountain of God.

verses.kjv: And he said unto Moses, I thy father in law Jethro am come unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.
verses.bsb: He sent word to Moses, “I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you with your wife and her two sons.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses went out to meet his father in law, and did obeisance, and kissed him; and they asked each other of their welfare; and they came into the tent.
verses.bsb: So Moses went out to meet his father-in-law and bowed down and kissed him. They greeted each other and went into the tent.

verses.kjv: And Moses told his father in law all that the Lord had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, and all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and how the Lord delivered them.
verses.bsb: Then Moses recounted to his father-in-law all that the LORD had done to Pharaoh and the Egyptians for Israel’s sake, all the hardships they had encountered along the way, and how the LORD had delivered them.

verses.kjv: And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the Lord had done to Israel, whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: And Jethro rejoiced over all the good things the LORD had done for Israel, whom He had rescued from the hand of the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: And Jethro said, Blessed be the Lord, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh, who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: Jethro declared, “Blessed be the LORD, who has delivered you from the hand of the Egyptians and of Pharaoh, and who has delivered the people from the hand of the Egyptians.

verses.kjv: Now I know that the Lord is greater than all gods: for in the thing wherein they dealt proudly he was above them.
verses.bsb: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all other gods, for He did this when they treated Israel with arrogance.”

verses.kjv: And Jethro, Moses’ father in law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God: and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses’ father in law before God.
verses.bsb: Then Moses’ father-in-law Jethro brought a burnt offering and sacrifices to God, and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses’ father-in-law in the presence of God.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people: and the people stood by Moses from the morning unto the evening.
verses.bsb: The next day Moses took his seat to judge the people, and they stood around him from morning until evening.

verses.kjv: And when Moses’ father in law saw all that he did to the people, he said, What is this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand by thee from morning unto even?
verses.bsb: When his father-in-law saw all that Moses was doing for the people, he asked, “What is this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone as judge, with all the people standing around you from morning till evening?”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto his father in law, Because the people come unto me to inquire of God:
verses.bsb: “Because the people come to me to inquire of God,” Moses replied.

verses.kjv: When they have a matter, they come unto me; and I judge between one and another, and I do make them know the statutes of God, and his laws.
verses.bsb: “Whenever they have a dispute, it is brought to me to judge between one man and another, and I make known to them the statutes and laws of God.”

verses.kjv: And Moses’ father in law said unto him, The thing that thou doest is not good.
verses.bsb: But Moses’ father-in-law said to him, “What you are doing is not good.

verses.kjv: Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that is with thee: for this thing is too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.
verses.bsb: Surely you and these people with you will wear yourselves out, because the task is too heavy for you. You cannot handle it alone.

verses.kjv: Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God shall be with thee: Be thou for the people to God-ward, that thou mayest bring the causes unto God:
verses.bsb: Now listen to me; I will give you some advice, and may God be with you. You must be the people’s representative before God and bring their causes to Him.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt teach them ordinances and laws, and shalt shew them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.
verses.bsb: Teach them the statutes and laws, and show them the way to live and the work they must do.

verses.kjv: Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, and rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens:
verses.bsb: Furthermore, select capable men from among the people—God-fearing, trustworthy men who are averse to dishonest gain. Appoint them over the people as leaders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.

verses.kjv: And let them judge the people at all seasons: and it shall be, that every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge: so shall it be easier for thyself, and they shall bear the burden with thee.
verses.bsb: Have these men judge the people at all times. Then they can bring you any major issue, but all minor cases they can judge on their own, so that your load may be lightened as they share it with you.

verses.kjv: If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee so, then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace.
verses.bsb: If you follow this advice and God so directs you, then you will be able to endure, and all these people can go home in peace.”

verses.kjv: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father in law, and did all that he had said.
verses.bsb: Moses listened to his father-in-law and did everything he said.

verses.kjv: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
verses.bsb: So Moses chose capable men from all Israel and made them heads over the people as leaders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens.

verses.kjv: And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.
verses.bsb: And they judged the people at all times; they would bring the difficult cases to Moses, but any minor issue they would judge themselves.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses let his father in law depart; and he went his way into his own land.
verses.bsb: Then Moses sent his father-in-law on his way, and Jethro returned to his own land.

verses.kjv: IN the third month, when the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they into the wilderness of Sinai.
verses.bsb: In the third month, on the same day of the month that the Israelites had left the land of Egypt, they came to the Wilderness of Sinai.

verses.kjv: For they were departed from Rephidim, and were come to the desert of Sinai, and had pitched in the wilderness; and there Israel camped before the mount.
verses.bsb: After they had set out from Rephidim, they entered the Wilderness of Sinai, and Israel camped there in front of the mountain.

verses.kjv: And Moses went up unto God, and the Lord called unto him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;
verses.bsb: Then Moses went up to God, and the LORD called to him from the mountain, “This is what you are to tell the house of Jacob and explain to the sons of Israel:

verses.kjv: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and how I bare you on eagles’ wings, and brought you unto myself.
verses.bsb: ‘You have seen for yourselves what I did to Egypt, and how I carried you on eagles’ wings and brought you to Myself.

verses.kjv: Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth is mine:
verses.bsb: Now if you will indeed obey My voice and keep My covenant, you will be My treasured possession out of all the nations—for the whole earth is Mine.

verses.kjv: And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation. These are the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: And unto Me you shall be a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.’ These are the words that you are to speak to the Israelites.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and laid before their faces all these words which the Lord commanded him.
verses.bsb: So Moses went back and summoned the elders of the people and set before them all these words that the LORD had commanded him.

verses.kjv: And all the people answered together, and said, All that the Lord hath spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: And all the people answered together, “We will do everything that the LORD has spoken.”  So Moses brought their words back to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: The LORD said to Moses, “Behold, I will come to you in a dense cloud, so that the people will hear when I speak with you, and they will always put their trust in you.”  And Moses relayed to the LORD what the people had said.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow. They must wash their clothes

verses.kjv: And be ready against the third day: for the third day the Lord will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.
verses.bsb: and be prepared by the third day, for on the third day the LORD will come down on Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, that ye go not up into the mount, or touch the border of it: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:
verses.bsb: And you are to set up a boundary for the people around the mountain and tell them, ‘Be careful not to go up on the mountain or touch its base. Whoever touches the mountain shall surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether it be beast or man, it shall not live: when the trumpet soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.
verses.bsb: No hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned or shot with arrows—whether man or beast, he must not live.’  Only when the ram’s horn sounds a long blast may they approach the mountain.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.
verses.bsb: When Moses came down from the mountain to the people, he consecrated them, and they washed their clothes.

verses.kjv: And he said unto the people, Be ready against the third day: come not at your wives.
verses.bsb: “Be prepared for the third day,” he said to the people. “Do not draw near to a woman.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the third day in the morning, that there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of the trumpet exceeding loud; so that all the people that was in the camp trembled.
verses.bsb: On the third day, when morning came, there was thunder and lightning. A thick cloud was upon the mountain, and a very loud blast of the ram’s horn went out, so that all the people in the camp trembled.

verses.kjv: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet with God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.
verses.bsb: Then Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet with God, and they stood at the foot of the mountain.

verses.kjv: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the Lord descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.
verses.bsb: Mount Sinai was completely enveloped in smoke, because the LORD had descended on it in fire. And the smoke rose like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mountain quaked violently.

verses.kjv: And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.
verses.bsb: And as the sound of the ram’s horn grew louder and louder, Moses spoke and God answered him in the thunder.

verses.kjv: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount: and the Lord called Moses up to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.
verses.bsb: The LORD descended to the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses to the summit. So Moses went up,

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the Lord to gaze, and many of them perish.
verses.bsb: and the LORD said to him, “Go down and warn the people not to break through to see the LORD, lest many of them perish.

verses.kjv: And let the priests also, which come near to the Lord, sanctify themselves, lest the Lord break forth upon them.
verses.bsb: Even the priests who approach the LORD must consecrate themselves, or the LORD will break out against them.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto the Lord, The people cannot come up to mount Sinai: for thou chargedst us, saying, Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.
verses.bsb: But Moses said to the LORD, “The people cannot come up Mount Sinai, for You solemnly warned us, ‘Put a boundary around the mountain and set it apart as holy.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Away, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee: but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the Lord, lest he break forth upon them.
verses.bsb: And the LORD replied, “Go down and bring Aaron with you. But the priests and the people must not break through to come up to the LORD, or He will break out against them.”

verses.kjv: So Moses went down unto the people, and spake unto them.
verses.bsb: So Moses went down to the people and spoke to them.

verses.kjv: AND God spake all these words, saying,
verses.bsb: And God spoke all these words:

verses.kjv: I am the Lord thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.
verses.bsb: “I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
verses.bsb: You shall have no other gods before Me.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth:
verses.bsb: You shall not make for yourself an idol in the form of anything in the heavens above, on the earth below, or in the waters beneath.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the Lord thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me;
verses.bsb: You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on their children to the third and fourth generations of those who hate Me,

verses.kjv: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me, and keep my commandments.
