verses.bsb: but showing loving devotion to a thousand generations of those who love Me and keep My commandments.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain; for the Lord will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
verses.bsb: You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not leave anyone unpunished who takes His name in vain.

verses.kjv: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.
verses.bsb: Remember the Sabbath day by keeping it holy.

verses.kjv: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:
verses.bsb: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,

verses.kjv: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the Lord thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates:
verses.bsb: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God, on which you must not do any work—neither you, nor your son or daughter, nor your manservant or maidservant or livestock, nor the foreigner within your gates.

verses.kjv: For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is, and rested the seventh day: wherefore the Lord blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.
verses.bsb: For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth and the sea and all that is in them, but on the seventh day He rested. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and set it apart as holy.

verses.kjv: ¶ Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
verses.bsb: Honor your father and mother, so that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not kill.
verses.bsb: You shall not murder.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not commit adultery.
verses.bsb: You shall not commit adultery.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not steal.
verses.bsb: You shall not steal.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
verses.bsb: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour’s.
verses.bsb: You shall not covet your neighbor’s house. You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or his manservant or maidservant, or his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And all the people saw the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the noise of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking: and when the people saw it, they removed, and stood afar off.
verses.bsb: When all the people witnessed the thunder and lightning, the sounding of the ram’s horn, and the mountain enveloped in smoke, they trembled and stood at a distance.

verses.kjv: And they said unto Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear: but let not God speak with us, lest we die.
verses.bsb: “Speak to us yourself and we will listen,” they said to Moses. “But do not let God speak to us, or we will die.”

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto the people, Fear not: for God is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before your faces, that ye sin not.
verses.bsb: “Do not be afraid,” Moses replied. “For God has come to test you, so that the fear of Him may be before you, to keep you from sinning.”

verses.kjv: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God was.
verses.bsb: And the people stood at a distance as Moses approached the thick darkness where God was.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel, Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “This is what you are to tell the Israelites: ‘You have seen for yourselves that I have spoken to you from heaven.

verses.kjv: Ye shall not make with me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold.
verses.bsb: You are not to make any gods alongside Me; you are not to make for yourselves gods of silver or gold.

verses.kjv: ¶ An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt offerings, and thy peace offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen: in all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee.
verses.bsb: You are to make for Me an altar of earth, and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and peace offerings, your sheep and goats and cattle. In every place where I cause My name to be remembered, I will come to you and bless you.

verses.kjv: And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it.
verses.bsb: Now if you make an altar of stones for Me, you must not build it with stones shaped by tools; for if you use a chisel on it, you will defile it.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.
verses.bsb: And you must not go up to My altar on steps, lest your nakedness be exposed on it.’

verses.kjv: NOW these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them.
verses.bsb: “These are the ordinances that you are to set before them:

verses.kjv: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.
verses.bsb: If you buy a Hebrew servant, he is to serve you for six years. But in the seventh year, he shall go free without paying anything.

verses.kjv: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.
verses.bsb: If he arrived alone, he is to leave alone; if he arrived with a wife, she is to leave with him.

verses.kjv: If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
verses.bsb: If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the woman and her children shall belong to her master, and only the man shall go free.

verses.kjv: And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free:
verses.bsb: But if the servant declares, ‘I love my master and my wife and children; I do not want to go free,’

verses.kjv: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever.
verses.bsb: then his master is to bring him before the judges. And he shall take him to the door or doorpost and pierce his ear with an awl. Then he shall serve his master for life.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.
verses.bsb: And if a man sells his daughter as a servant, she is not to go free as the menservants do.

verses.kjv: If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.
verses.bsb: If she is displeasing in the eyes of her master who had designated her for himself, he must allow her to be redeemed. He has no right to sell her to foreigners, since he has broken faith with her.

verses.kjv: And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
verses.bsb: And if he chooses her for his son, he must deal with her as with a daughter.

verses.kjv: If he take him another wife; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.
verses.bsb: If he takes another wife, he must not reduce the food, clothing, or marital rights of his first wife.

verses.kjv: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money.
verses.bsb: If, however, he does not provide her with these three things, she is free to go without monetary payment.

verses.kjv: ¶ He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.
verses.bsb: Whoever strikes and kills a man must surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver him into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
verses.bsb: If, however, he did not lie in wait, but God allowed it to happen, then I will appoint for you a place where he may flee.

verses.kjv: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.
verses.bsb: But if a man schemes and acts willfully against his neighbor to kill him, you must take him away from My altar to be put to death.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.
verses.bsb: Whoever strikes his father or mother must surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: Whoever kidnaps another man must be put to death, whether he sells him or the man is found in his possession.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: Anyone who curses his father or mother must surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with his fist, and he die not, but keepeth his bed:
verses.bsb: If men are quarreling and one strikes the other with a stone or a fist, and he does not die but is confined to bed,

verses.kjv: If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote him be quit: only he shall pay for the loss of his time, and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed.
verses.bsb: then the one who struck him shall go unpunished, as long as the other can get up and walk around outside with his staff. Nevertheless, he must compensate the man for his lost work and see that he is completely healed.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished.
verses.bsb: If a man strikes his manservant or maidservant with a rod, and the servant dies by his hand, he shall surely be punished.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he is his money.
verses.bsb: However, if the servant gets up after a day or two, the owner shall not be punished, since the servant is his property.

verses.kjv: ¶ If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart from her, and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman’s husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges determine.
verses.bsb: If men who are fighting strike a pregnant woman and her child is born prematurely, but there is no further injury, he shall surely be fined as the woman’s husband demands and as the court allows.

verses.kjv: And if any mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,
verses.bsb: But if a serious injury results, then you must require a life for a life—

verses.kjv: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
verses.bsb: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

verses.kjv: Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.
verses.bsb: burn for burn, wound for wound, and stripe for stripe.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
verses.bsb: If a man strikes and blinds the eye of his manservant or maidservant, he must let the servant go free as compensation for the eye.

verses.kjv: And if he smite out his manservant’s tooth, or his maidservant’s tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
verses.bsb: And if he knocks out the tooth of his manservant or maidservant, he must let the servant go free as compensation for the tooth.

verses.kjv: ¶ If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox shall be quit.
verses.bsb: If an ox gores a man or woman to death, the ox must surely be stoned, and its meat must not be eaten. But the owner of the ox shall not be held responsible.

verses.kjv: But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past, and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.
verses.bsb: But if the ox has a habit of goring, and its owner has been warned yet does not restrain it, and it kills a man or woman, then the ox must be stoned and its owner must also be put to death.

verses.kjv: If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
verses.bsb: If payment is demanded of him instead, he may redeem his life by paying the full amount demanded of him.

verses.kjv: Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.
verses.bsb: If the ox gores a son or a daughter, it shall be done to him according to the same rule.

verses.kjv: If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.
verses.bsb: If the ox gores a manservant or maidservant, the owner must pay thirty shekels of silver to the master of that servant, and the ox must be stoned.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;
verses.bsb: If a man opens or digs a pit and fails to cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it,

verses.kjv: The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.
verses.bsb: the owner of the pit shall make restitution; he must pay its owner, and the dead animal will be his.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if one man’s ox hurt another’s, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead ox also they shall divide.
verses.bsb: If a man’s ox injures his neighbor’s ox and it dies, they must sell the live one and divide the proceeds; they also must divide the dead animal.

verses.kjv: Or if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past, and his owner hath not kept him in; he shall surely pay ox for ox; and the dead shall be his own.
verses.bsb: But if it was known that the ox had a habit of goring, yet its owner failed to restrain it, he shall pay full compensation, ox for ox, and the dead animal will be his.

verses.kjv: IF a man shall steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.
verses.bsb: “If a man steals an ox or a sheep and slaughters or sells it, he must repay five oxen for an ox and four sheep for a sheep.

verses.kjv: ¶ If a thief be found breaking up, and be smitten that he die, there shall no blood be shed for him.
verses.bsb: If a thief is caught breaking in and is beaten to death, no one shall be guilty of bloodshed.

verses.kjv: If the sun be risen upon him, there shall be blood shed for him; for he should make full restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.
verses.bsb: But if it happens after sunrise, there is guilt for his bloodshed.  A thief must make full restitution; if he has nothing, he himself shall be sold for his theft.

verses.kjv: If the theft be certainly found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep; he shall restore double.
verses.bsb: If what was stolen is actually found alive in his possession—whether ox or donkey or sheep—he must pay back double.

verses.kjv: ¶ If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall put in his beast, and shall feed in another man’s field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.
verses.bsb: If a man grazes his livestock in a field or vineyard and allows them to stray so that they graze in someone else’s field, he must make restitution from the best of his own field or vineyard.

verses.kjv: ¶ If fire break out, and catch in thorns, so that the stacks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field, be consumed therewith; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.
verses.bsb: If a fire breaks out and spreads to thornbushes so that it consumes stacked or standing grain, or the whole field, the one who started the fire must make full restitution.

verses.kjv: ¶ If a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief be found, let him pay double.
verses.bsb: If a man gives his neighbor money or goods for safekeeping and they are stolen from the neighbor’s house, the thief, if caught, must pay back double.

verses.kjv: If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges, to see whether he have put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods.
verses.bsb: If the thief is not found, the owner of the house must appear before the judges to determine whether he has taken his neighbor’s property.

verses.kjv: For all manner of trespass, whether it be for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, or for any manner of lost thing, which another challengeth to be his, the cause of both parties shall come before the judges; and whom the judges shall condemn, he shall pay double unto his neighbour.
verses.bsb: In all cases of illegal possession of an ox, a donkey, a sheep, a garment, or any lost item that someone claims, ‘This is mine,’ both parties shall bring their cases before the judges. The one whom the judges find guilty must pay back double to his neighbor.

verses.kjv: If a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep; and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing it:
verses.bsb: If a man gives a donkey, an ox, a sheep, or any other animal to be cared for by his neighbor, but it dies or is injured or stolen while no one is watching,

verses.kjv: Then shall an oath of the Lord be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour’s goods; and the owner of it shall accept thereof, and he shall not make it good.
verses.bsb: an oath before the LORD shall be made between the parties to determine whether or not the man has taken his neighbor’s property. The owner must accept the oath and require no restitution.

verses.kjv: And if it be stolen from him, he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof.
verses.bsb: But if the animal was actually stolen from the neighbor, he must make restitution to the owner.

verses.kjv: If it be torn in pieces, then let him bring it for witness, and he shall not make good that which was torn.
verses.bsb: If the animal was torn to pieces, he shall bring it as evidence; he need not make restitution for the torn carcass.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man borrow ought of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof being not with it, he shall surely make it good.
verses.bsb: If a man borrows an animal from his neighbor and it is injured or dies while its owner is not present, he must make full restitution.

verses.kjv: But if the owner thereof be with it, he shall not make it good: if it be an hired thing, it came for his hire.
verses.bsb: If the owner was present, no restitution is required. If the animal was rented, the fee covers the loss.

verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man entice a maid that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely endow her to be his wife.
verses.bsb: If a man seduces a virgin who is not pledged in marriage and sleeps with her, he must pay the full dowry for her to be his wife.

verses.kjv: If her father utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.
verses.bsb: If her father absolutely refuses to give her to him, the man still must pay an amount comparable to the bridal price of a virgin.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.
verses.bsb: You must not allow a sorceress to live.

verses.kjv: ¶ Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: Whoever lies with an animal must surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: ¶ He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the Lord only, he shall be utterly destroyed.
verses.bsb: If anyone sacrifices to any god other than the LORD alone, he must be set apart for destruction.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: You must not exploit or oppress a foreign resident, for you yourselves were foreigners in the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall not afflict any widow, or fatherless child.
verses.bsb: You must not mistreat any widow or orphan.

verses.kjv: If thou afflict them in any wise, and they cry at all unto me, I will surely hear their cry;
verses.bsb: If you do mistreat them, and they cry out to Me in distress, I will surely hear their cry.

verses.kjv: And my wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.
verses.bsb: My anger will be kindled, and I will kill you with the sword; then your wives will become widows and your children will be fatherless.

verses.kjv: ¶ If thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee, thou shalt not be to him as an usurer, neither shalt thou lay upon him usury.
verses.bsb: If you lend money to one of My people among you who is poor, you must not act as a creditor to him; you are not to charge him interest.

verses.kjv: If thou at all take thy neighbour’s raiment to pledge, thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down:
verses.bsb: If you take your neighbor’s cloak as collateral, return it to him by sunset,

verses.kjv: For that is his covering only, it is his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.
verses.bsb: because his cloak is the only covering he has for his body. What else will he sleep in? And if he cries out to Me, I will hear, for I am compassionate.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not revile the gods, nor curse the ruler of thy people.
verses.bsb: You must not blaspheme God or curse the ruler of your people.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not delay to offer the first of thy ripe fruits, and of thy liquors: the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.
verses.bsb: You must not hold back offerings from your granaries or vats. You are to give Me the firstborn of your sons.

verses.kjv: Likewise shalt thou do with thine oxen, and with thy sheep: seven days it shall be with his dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it me.
verses.bsb: You shall do likewise with your cattle and your sheep. Let them stay with their mothers for seven days, but on the eighth day you are to give them to Me.

verses.kjv: ¶ And ye shall be holy men unto me: neither shall ye eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs.
verses.bsb: You are to be My holy people. You must not eat the meat of a mauled animal found in the field; you are to throw it to the dogs.

verses.kjv: THOU shalt not raise a false report: put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.
verses.bsb: “You shall not spread a false report. Do not join the wicked by being a malicious witness.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil; neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest judgment:
verses.bsb: You shall not follow the crowd in wrongdoing. When you testify in a lawsuit, do not pervert justice by siding with the crowd.

verses.kjv: ¶ Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause.
verses.bsb: And do not show favoritism to a poor man in his lawsuit.

verses.kjv: ¶ If thou meet thine enemy’s ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.
verses.bsb: If you encounter your enemy’s stray ox or donkey, you must return it to him.

verses.kjv: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, and wouldest forbear to help him, thou shalt surely help with him.
verses.bsb: If you see the donkey of one who hates you fallen under its load, do not leave it there; you must help him with it.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.
verses.bsb: You shall not deny justice to the poor in their lawsuits.

verses.kjv: Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.
verses.bsb: Stay far away from a false accusation. Do not kill the innocent or the just, for I will not acquit the guilty.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt take no gift: for the gift blindeth the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous.
verses.bsb: Do not accept a bribe, for a bribe blinds those who see and twists the words of the righteous.

verses.kjv: ¶ Also thou shalt not oppress a stranger: for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: Do not oppress a foreign resident, since you yourselves know how it feels to be foreigners; for you were foreigners in the land of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and shalt gather in the fruits thereof:
verses.bsb: For six years you are to sow your land and gather its produce,

verses.kjv: But the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still; that the poor of thy people may eat: and what they leave the beasts of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, and with thy oliveyard.
verses.bsb: but in the seventh year you must let it rest and lie fallow, so that the poor among your people may eat from the field and the wild animals may consume what they leave. Do the same with your vineyard and olive grove.

verses.kjv: Six days thou shalt do thy work, and on the seventh day thou shalt rest: that thine ox and thine ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed.
verses.bsb: For six days you are to do your work, but on the seventh day you must cease, so that your ox and your donkey may rest and the son of your maidservant may be refreshed, as well as the foreign resident.

verses.kjv: And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect: and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth.
verses.bsb: Pay close attention to everything I have said to you. You must not invoke the names of other gods; they must not be heard on your lips.

verses.kjv: ¶ Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year.
verses.bsb: Three times a year you are to celebrate a feast to Me.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread: (thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days, as I commanded thee, in the time appointed of the month Abib; for in it thou camest out from Egypt: and none shall appear before me empty:)
verses.bsb: You are to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread as I commanded you: At the appointed time in the month of Abib you are to eat unleavened bread for seven days, because that was the month you came out of Egypt. No one may appear before Me empty-handed.

verses.kjv: And the feast of harvest, the firstfruits of thy labours, which thou hast sown in the field: and the feast of ingathering, which is in the end of the year, when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field.
verses.bsb: You are also to keep the Feast of Harvest with the firstfruits of the produce from what you sow in the field.  And keep the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather your produce from the field.

verses.kjv: Three times in the year all thy males shall appear before the Lord God.
verses.bsb: Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning.
verses.bsb: You must not offer the blood of My sacrifices with anything leavened, nor may the fat of My feast remain until morning.

verses.kjv: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
verses.bsb: Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the LORD your God.  You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.

verses.kjv: ¶ Behold, I send an Angel before thee, to keep thee in the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.
verses.bsb: Behold, I am sending an angel before you to protect you along the way and to bring you to the place I have prepared.

verses.kjv: Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not; for he will not pardon your transgressions: for my name is in him.
verses.bsb: Pay attention to him and listen to his voice; do not defy him, for he will not forgive rebellion, since My Name is in him.

verses.kjv: But if thou shalt indeed obey his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.
verses.bsb: But if you will listen carefully to his voice and do everything I say, I will be an enemy to your enemies and a foe to your foes.

verses.kjv: For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites: and I will cut them off.
verses.bsb: For My angel will go before you and bring you into the land of the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites, and I will annihilate them.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works: but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and quite break down their images.
verses.bsb: You must not bow down to their gods or serve them or follow their practices. Instead, you are to demolish them and smash their sacred stones to pieces.

verses.kjv: And ye shall serve the Lord your God, and he shall bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee.
verses.bsb: So you shall serve the LORD your God, and He will bless your bread and your water. And I will take away sickness from among you.

verses.kjv: ¶ There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land: the number of thy days I will fulfil.
verses.bsb: No woman in your land will miscarry or be barren; I will fulfill the number of your days.

verses.kjv: I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.
verses.bsb: I will send My terror ahead of you and throw into confusion every nation you encounter. I will make all your enemies turn and run.

verses.kjv: And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.
verses.bsb: I will send the hornet before you to drive the Hivites and Canaanites and Hittites out of your way.

verses.kjv: I will not drive them out from before thee in one year; lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.
verses.bsb: I will not drive them out before you in a single year; otherwise the land would become desolate and wild animals would multiply against you.

verses.kjv: By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.
verses.bsb: Little by little I will drive them out ahead of you, until you become fruitful and possess the land.

verses.kjv: And I will set thy bounds from the Red sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the desert unto the river: for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.
verses.bsb: And I will establish your borders from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines, and from the desert to the Euphrates. For I will deliver the inhabitants into your hand, and you will drive them out before you.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.
verses.bsb: You shall make no covenant with them or with their gods.

verses.kjv: They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me: for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee.
verses.bsb: They must not remain in your land, lest they cause you to sin against Me. For if you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.”

verses.kjv: AND he said unto Moses, Come up unto the Lord, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Come up to the LORD—you and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of Israel’s elders—and you are to worship at a distance.

verses.kjv: And Moses alone shall come near the Lord: but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people go up with him.
verses.bsb: Moses alone shall approach the LORD, but the others must not come near. And the people may not go up with him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses came and told the people all the words of the Lord, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words which the Lord hath said will we do.
verses.bsb: When Moses came and told the people all the words and ordinances of the LORD, they all responded with one voice: “All the words that the LORD has spoken, we will do.”

verses.kjv: And Moses wrote all the words of the Lord, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.bsb: And Moses wrote down all the words of the LORD.  Early the next morning he got up and built an altar at the base of the mountain, along with twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.kjv: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, which offered burnt offerings, and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then he sent out some young men of Israel, and they offered burnt offerings and sacrificed young bulls as peace offerings to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And Moses took half of the blood, and put it in basons; and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.
verses.bsb: Moses took half of the blood and put it in bowls, and the other half he sprinkled on the altar.

verses.kjv: And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the Lord hath said will we do, and be obedient.
verses.bsb: Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it to the people, who replied, “All that the LORD has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.”

verses.kjv: And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant, which the Lord hath made with you concerning all these words.
verses.bsb: So Moses took the blood, sprinkled it on the people, and said, “This is the blood of the covenant that the LORD has made with you in accordance with all these words.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then went up Moses, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel:
verses.bsb: Then Moses went up with Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel,

verses.kjv: And they saw the God of Israel: and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone, and as it were the body of heaven in his clearness.
verses.bsb: and they saw the God of Israel. Under His feet was a work like a pavement made of sapphire, as clear as the sky itself.

verses.kjv: And upon the nobles of the children of Israel he laid not his hand: also they saw God, and did eat and drink.
verses.bsb: But God did not lay His hand on the nobles of Israel; they saw Him, and they ate and drank.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and a law, and commandments which I have written; that thou mayest teach them.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Come up to Me on the mountain and stay here, so that I may give you the tablets of stone, with the law and commandments I have written for their instruction.”

verses.kjv: And Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua: and Moses went up into the mount of God.
verses.bsb: So Moses set out with Joshua his attendant and went up on the mountain of God.

verses.kjv: And he said unto the elders, Tarry ye here for us, until we come again unto you: and, behold, Aaron and Hur are with you: if any man have any matters to do, let him come unto them.
verses.bsb: And he said to the elders, “Wait here for us until we return to you. Aaron and Hur are here with you. Whoever has a dispute can go to them.”

verses.kjv: And Moses went up into the mount, and a cloud covered the mount.
verses.bsb: When Moses went up on the mountain, the cloud covered it,

verses.kjv: And the glory of the Lord abode upon mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days: and the seventh day he called unto Moses out of the midst of the cloud.
verses.bsb: and the glory of the LORD settled on Mount Sinai. For six days the cloud covered it, and on the seventh day the LORD called to Moses from within the cloud.

verses.kjv: And the sight of the glory of the Lord was like devouring fire on the top of the mount in the eyes of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: And the sight of the glory of the LORD was like a consuming fire on the mountaintop in the eyes of the Israelites.

verses.kjv: And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and gat him up into the mount: and Moses was in the mount forty days and forty nights.
verses.bsb: Moses entered the cloud as he went up on the mountain, and he remained on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering.
verses.bsb: “Tell the Israelites to bring Me an offering. You are to receive My offering from every man whose heart compels him.

verses.kjv: And this is the offering which ye shall take of them; gold, and silver, and brass,
verses.bsb: This is the offering you are to accept from them:  gold, silver, and bronze;

verses.kjv: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair,
verses.bsb: blue, purple, and scarlet yarn;   fine linen and goat hair;

verses.kjv: And rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and shittim wood,
verses.bsb: ram skins dyed red and fine leather;   acacia wood;

verses.kjv: Oil for the light, spices for anointing oil, and for sweet incense,
verses.bsb: olive oil for the light;   spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;

verses.kjv: Onyx stones, and stones to be set in the ephod, and in the breastplate.
verses.bsb: and onyx stones and gemstones to be mounted on the ephod and breastpiece.

verses.kjv: And let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.
verses.bsb: And they are to make a sanctuary for Me, so that I may dwell among them.

verses.kjv: According to all that I shew thee, after the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the instruments thereof, even so shall ye make it.
verses.bsb: You must make the tabernacle and design all its furnishings according to the pattern I show you.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they shall make an ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.
verses.bsb: And they are to construct an ark of acacia wood, two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, within and without shalt thou overlay it, and shalt make upon it a crown of gold round about.
verses.bsb: Overlay it with pure gold both inside and out, and make a gold molding around it.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in the four corners thereof; and two rings shall be in the one side of it, and two rings in the other side of it.
verses.bsb: Cast four gold rings for it and fasten them to its four feet, two rings on one side and two on the other.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
verses.bsb: And make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.
verses.bsb: Insert the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, in order to carry it.

verses.kjv: The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not be taken from it.
verses.bsb: The poles are to remain in the rings of the ark; they must not be removed.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.
verses.bsb: And place inside the ark the Testimony, which I will give you.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.
verses.bsb: And you are to construct a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make two cherubims of gold, of beaten work shalt thou make them, in the two ends of the mercy seat.
verses.bsb: Make two cherubim of hammered gold at the ends of the mercy seat,

verses.kjv: And make one cherub on the one end, and the other cherub on the other end: even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof.
verses.bsb: one cherub on one end and one on the other, all made from one piece of gold.

verses.kjv: And the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces shall look one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be.
verses.bsb: And the cherubim are to have wings that spread upward, overshadowing the mercy seat. The cherubim are to face each other, looking toward the mercy seat.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark; and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that I shall give thee.
verses.bsb: Set the mercy seat atop the ark, and put the Testimony that I will give you into the ark.

verses.kjv: And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims which are upon the ark of the testimony, of all things which I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: And I will meet with you there above the mercy seat, between the two cherubim that are over the ark of the Testimony; I will speak with you about all that I command you regarding the Israelites.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt also make a table of shittim wood: two cubits shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.
verses.bsb: You are also to make a table of acacia wood two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, and make thereto a crown of gold round about.
verses.bsb: Overlay it with pure gold and make a gold molding around it.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make unto it a border of an hand breadth round about, and thou shalt make a golden crown to the border thereof round about.
verses.bsb: And make a rim around it a handbreadth wide and put a gold molding on the rim.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make for it four rings of gold, and put the rings in the four corners that are on the four feet thereof.
verses.bsb: Make four gold rings for the table and fasten them to the four corners at its four legs.

verses.kjv: Over against the border shall the rings be for places of the staves to bear the table.
verses.bsb: The rings are to be close to the rim, to serve as holders for the poles used to carry the table.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be borne with them.
verses.bsb: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold, so that the table may be carried with them.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and spoons thereof, and covers thereof, and bowls thereof, to cover withal: of pure gold shalt thou make them.
verses.bsb: You are also to make the plates and dishes, as well as the pitchers and bowls for pouring drink offerings. Make them out of pure gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway.
verses.bsb: And place the Bread of the Presence on the table before Me at all times.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work shall the candlestick be made: his shaft, and his branches, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, shall be of the same.
verses.bsb: Then you are to make a lampstand of pure, hammered gold. It shall be made of one piece, including its base and shaft, its cups, and its buds and petals.

verses.kjv: And six branches shall come out of the sides of it; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side:
verses.bsb: Six branches are to extend from the sides of the lampstand—three on one side and three on the other.

verses.kjv: Three bowls made like unto almonds, with a knop and a flower in one branch; and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch, with a knop and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick.
verses.bsb: There are to be three cups shaped like almond blossoms on the first branch, each with buds and petals, three on the next branch, and the same for all six branches that extend from the lampstand.

verses.kjv: And in the candlestick shall be four bowls made like unto almonds, with their knops and their flowers.
verses.bsb: And on the lampstand there shall be four cups shaped like almond blossoms with buds and petals.

verses.kjv: And there shall be a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick.
verses.bsb: For the six branches that extend from the lampstand, a bud must be under the first pair of branches, a bud under the second pair, and a bud under the third pair.

verses.kjv: Their knops and their branches shall be of the same: all it shall be one beaten work of pure gold.
verses.bsb: The buds and branches are to be all of one piece with the lampstand, hammered out of pure gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof: and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may give light over against it.
verses.bsb: Make seven lamps and set them up on the lampstand so that they illuminate the area in front of it.

verses.kjv: And the tongs thereof, and the snuffdishes thereof, shall be of pure gold.
verses.bsb: The wick trimmers and their trays must be of pure gold.

verses.kjv: Of a talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.
verses.bsb: The lampstand and all these utensils shall be made from a talent of pure gold.

verses.kjv: And look that thou make them after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount.
verses.bsb: See to it that you make everything according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.

verses.kjv: MOREOVER thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them.
verses.bsb: “You are to construct the tabernacle itself with ten curtains of finely spun linen, each with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and cherubim skillfully worked into them.

verses.kjv: The length of one curtain shall be eight and twenty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and every one of the curtains shall have one measure.
verses.bsb: Each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide—all curtains the same size.

verses.kjv: The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another.
verses.bsb: Five of the curtains are to be joined together, and the other five joined as well.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.
verses.bsb: Make loops of blue material on the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and do the same for the end curtain in the second set.

verses.kjv: Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that is in the coupling of the second; that the loops may take hold one of another.
verses.bsb: Make fifty loops on one curtain and fifty loops on the end curtain of the second set, so that the loops line up opposite one another.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make fifty taches of gold, and couple the curtains together with the taches: and it shall be one tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Make fifty gold clasps as well, and join the curtains together with the clasps, so that the tabernacle will be a unit.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make curtains of goats’ hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make.
verses.bsb: You are to make curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven curtains in all.

verses.kjv: The length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and the eleven curtains shall be all of one measure.
verses.bsb: Each of the eleven curtains is to be the same size—thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Join five of the curtains into one set and the other six into another. Then fold the sixth curtain over double at the front of the tent.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outmost in the coupling, and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.
verses.bsb: Make fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make fifty taches of brass, and put the taches into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.
verses.bsb: Make fifty bronze clasps and put them through the loops to join the tent together as a unit.

verses.kjv: And the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: As for the overlap that remains of the tent curtains, the half curtain that is left over shall hang down over the back of the tabernacle.

verses.kjv: And a cubit on the one side, and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, it shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.
verses.bsb: And the tent curtains will be a cubit longer on either side, and the excess will hang over the sides of the tabernacle to cover it.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering above of badgers’ skins.
verses.bsb: Also make a covering for the tent out of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of fine leather.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up.
verses.bsb: You are to construct upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.

verses.kjv: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board.
verses.bsb: Each frame is to be ten cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.

verses.kjv: Two tenons shall there be in one board, set in order one against another: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Two tenons must be connected to each other for each frame. Make all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.
verses.bsb: Construct twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
verses.bsb: with forty silver bases under the twenty frames—two bases for each frame, one under each tenon.

verses.kjv: And for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards:
verses.bsb: For the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, make twenty frames

verses.kjv: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
verses.bsb: and forty silver bases—two bases under each frame.

verses.kjv: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.
verses.bsb: Make six frames for the rear of the tabernacle, the west side,

verses.kjv: And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
verses.bsb: and two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.
verses.bsb: coupled together from bottom to top and fitted into a single ring. These will serve as the two corners.

verses.kjv: And they shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
verses.bsb: So there are to be eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
verses.bsb: You are also to make five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the two sides westward.
verses.bsb: five for those on the other side, and five for those on the rear side of the tabernacle, to the west.

verses.kjv: And the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end.
verses.bsb: The central crossbar in the middle of the frames shall extend from one end to the other.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.
verses.bsb: Overlay the frames with gold and make gold rings to hold the crossbars. Also overlay the crossbars with gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the fashion thereof which was shewed thee in the mount.
verses.bsb: So you are to set up the tabernacle according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen of cunning work: with cherubims shall it be made:
verses.bsb: Make a veil of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen, with cherubim skillfully worked into it.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim wood overlaid with gold: their hooks shall be of gold, upon the four sockets of silver.
verses.bsb: Hang it with gold hooks on four posts of acacia wood, overlaid with gold and standing on four silver bases.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt hang up the vail under the taches, that thou mayest bring in thither within the vail the ark of the testimony: and the vail shall divide unto you between the holy place and the most holy.
verses.bsb: And hang the veil from the clasps and place the ark of the Testimony behind the veil. So the veil will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
verses.bsb: Put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and thou shalt put the table on the north side.
verses.bsb: And place the table outside the veil on the north side of the tabernacle, and put the lampstand opposite the table, on the south side.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework.
verses.bsb: For the entrance to the tent, you are to make a curtain embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make for the hanging five pillars of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, and their hooks shall be of gold: and thou shalt cast five sockets of brass for them.
verses.bsb: Make five posts of acacia wood for the curtain, overlay them with gold hooks, and cast five bronze bases for them.

verses.kjv: AND thou shalt make an altar of shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof shall be three cubits.
verses.bsb: “You are to build an altar of acacia wood. The altar must be square, five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the horns of it upon the four corners thereof: his horns shall be of the same: and thou shalt overlay it with brass.
verses.bsb: Make a horn on each of its four corners, so that the horns are of one piece, and overlay it with bronze.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basons, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make of brass.
verses.bsb: Make all its utensils of bronze—its pots for removing ashes, its shovels, its sprinkling bowls, its meat forks, and its firepans.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make for it a grate of network of brass; and upon the net shalt thou make four brasen rings in the four corners thereof.
verses.bsb: Construct for it a grate of bronze mesh, and make a bronze ring at each of the four corners of the mesh.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.
verses.bsb: Set the grate beneath the ledge of the altar, so that the mesh comes halfway up the altar.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with brass.
verses.bsb: Additionally, make poles of acacia wood for the altar and overlay them with bronze.

verses.kjv: And the staves shall be put into the rings, and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar, to bear it.
verses.bsb: The poles are to be inserted into the rings so that the poles are on two sides of the altar when it is carried.

verses.kjv: Hollow with boards shalt thou make it: as it was shewed thee in the mount, so shall they make it.
verses.bsb: Construct the altar with boards so that it is hollow. It is to be made just as you were shown on the mountain.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen of an hundred cubits long for one side:
verses.bsb: You are also to make a courtyard for the tabernacle. On the south side of the courtyard make curtains of finely spun linen, a hundred cubits long on one side,

verses.kjv: And the twenty pillars thereof and their twenty sockets shall be of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets shall be of silver.
verses.bsb: with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases, and silver hooks and bands on the posts.

verses.kjv: And likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings of an hundred cubits long, and his twenty pillars and their twenty sockets of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver.
verses.bsb: Likewise there are to be curtains on the north side, a hundred cubits long, with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases, and with silver hooks and bands on the posts.

verses.kjv: ¶ And for the breadth of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits: their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.
verses.bsb: The curtains on the west side of the courtyard shall be fifty cubits wide, with ten posts and ten bases.

verses.kjv: And the breadth of the court on the east side eastward shall be fifty cubits.
verses.bsb: The east side of the courtyard, toward the sunrise, is to be fifty cubits wide.

verses.kjv: The hangings of one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.
verses.bsb: Make the curtains on one side fifteen cubits long, with three posts and three bases,

verses.kjv: And on the other side shall be hangings fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.
verses.bsb: and the curtains on the other side fifteen cubits long, with three posts and three bases.

verses.kjv: ¶ And for the gate of the court shall be an hanging of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework: and their pillars shall be four, and their sockets four.
verses.bsb: The gate of the courtyard shall be twenty cubits long, with a curtain embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen. It shall have four posts and four bases.

verses.kjv: All the pillars round about the court shall be filleted with silver; their hooks shall be of silver, and their sockets of brass.
verses.bsb: All the posts around the courtyard shall have silver bands, silver hooks, and bronze bases.

verses.kjv: ¶ The length of the court shall be an hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty every where, and the height five cubits of fine twined linen, and their sockets of brass.
verses.bsb: The entire courtyard shall be a hundred cubits long and fifty cubits wide, with curtains of finely spun linen five cubits high, and with bronze bases.

verses.kjv: All the vessels of the tabernacle in all the service thereof, and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, shall be of brass.
verses.bsb: All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use, including all its tent pegs and the tent pegs of the courtyard, shall be made of bronze.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn always.
verses.bsb: And you are to command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of pressed olives for the light, to keep the lamps burning continually.

verses.kjv: In the tabernacle of the congregation without the vail, which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall order it from evening to morning before the Lord: it shall be a statute for ever unto their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: In the Tent of Meeting, outside the veil that is in front of the Testimony, Aaron and his sons are to tend the lamps before the LORD from evening until morning. This is to be a permanent statute for the Israelites for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: AND take thou unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
verses.bsb: “Next, have your brother Aaron brought to you from among the Israelites, along with his sons Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar, to serve Me as priests.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother for glory and for beauty.
verses.bsb: Make holy garments for your brother Aaron, to give him glory and splendor.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt speak unto all that are wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron’s garments to consecrate him, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: You are to instruct all the skilled craftsmen, whom I have filled with a spirit of wisdom, to make garments for Aaron’s consecration, so that he may serve Me as priest.

verses.kjv: And these are the garments which they shall make; a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre, and a girdle: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a woven tunic, a turban, and a sash. They are to make these holy garments for your brother Aaron and his sons, so that they may serve Me as priests.

verses.kjv: And they shall take gold, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen.
verses.bsb: They shall use gold, along with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine linen.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and of purple, of scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cunning work.
verses.bsb: They are to make the ephod of finely spun linen embroidered with gold, and with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn.

verses.kjv: It shall have the two shoulderpieces thereof joined at the two edges thereof; and so it shall be joined together.
verses.bsb: It shall have two shoulder pieces attached at two of its corners, so it can be fastened.

verses.kjv: And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, shall be of the same, according to the work thereof; even of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
verses.bsb: And the skillfully woven waistband of the ephod must be of one piece, of the same workmanship—with gold, with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and with finely spun linen.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and grave on them the names of the children of Israel:
verses.bsb: Take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:

verses.kjv: Six of their names on one stone, and the other six names of the rest on the other stone, according to their birth.
verses.bsb: six of their names on one stone and the remaining six on the other, in the order of their birth.

verses.kjv: With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel: thou shalt make them to be set in ouches of gold.
verses.bsb: Engrave the names of the sons of Israel on the two stones the way a gem cutter engraves a seal. Then mount the stones in gold filigree settings.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulders of the ephod for stones of memorial unto the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before the Lord upon his two shoulders for a memorial.
verses.bsb: Fasten both stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. Aaron is to bear their names on his two shoulders as a memorial before the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make ouches of gold;
verses.bsb: Fashion gold filigree settings

verses.kjv: And two chains of pure gold at the ends; of wreathen work shalt thou make them, and fasten the wreathen chains to the ouches.
verses.bsb: and two chains of pure gold, made of braided cord work; and attach these chains to the settings.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make the breastplate of judgment with cunning work; after the work of the ephod thou shalt make it; of gold, of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.
verses.bsb: You are also to make a breastpiece of judgment with the same workmanship as the ephod. Construct it with gold, with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and with finely spun linen.

verses.kjv: Foursquare it shall be being doubled; a span shall be the length thereof, and a span shall be the breadth thereof.
verses.bsb: It must be square when folded over double, a span long and a span wide.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt set in it settings of stones, even four rows of stones: the first row shall be a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: this shall be the first row.
verses.bsb: And mount on it a setting of gemstones, four rows of stones:  In the first row there shall be a ruby, a topaz, and an emerald;

verses.kjv: And the second row shall be an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.
verses.bsb: in the second row a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond;

verses.kjv: And the third row a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.
verses.bsb: in the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;

verses.kjv: And the fourth row a beryl, and an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be set in gold in their inclosings.
verses.bsb: and in the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper.   Mount these stones in gold filigree settings.

verses.kjv: And the stones shall be with the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, like the engravings of a signet; every one with his name shall they be according to the twelve tribes.
verses.bsb: The twelve stones are to correspond to the names of the sons of Israel, each engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make upon the breastplate chains at the ends of wreathen work of pure gold.
verses.bsb: For the breastpiece, make braided chains like cords of pure gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make upon the breastplate two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
verses.bsb: You are also to make two gold rings and fasten them to the two corners of the breastpiece.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings which are on the ends of the breastplate.
verses.bsb: Then fasten the two gold chains to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece,

verses.kjv: And the other two ends of the two wreathen chains thou shalt fasten in the two ouches, and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod before it.
verses.bsb: and fasten the other ends of the two chains to the two filigree settings, attaching them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the breastplate in the border thereof, which is in the side of the ephod inward.
verses.bsb: Make two more gold rings and attach them to the other two corners of the breastpiece, on the inside edge next to the ephod.

verses.kjv: And two other rings of gold thou shalt make, and shalt put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart thereof, over against the other coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.
verses.bsb: Make two additional gold rings and attach them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod, on its front, near its seam just above its woven waistband.

verses.kjv: And they shall bind the breastplate by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.
verses.bsb: The rings of the breastpiece shall be tied to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue yarn, so that the breastpiece is above the waistband of the ephod and does not swing out from the ephod.

verses.kjv: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy place, for a memorial before the Lord continually.
verses.bsb: Whenever Aaron enters the Holy Place, he shall bear the names of the sons of Israel over his heart on the breastpiece of judgment, as a continual reminder before the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron’s heart, when he goeth in before the Lord: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the Lord continually.
verses.bsb: And place the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece of judgment, so that they will also be over Aaron’s heart whenever he comes before the LORD. Aaron will continually carry the judgment of the sons of Israel over his heart before the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
verses.bsb: You are to make the robe of the ephod entirely of blue cloth,

verses.kjv: And there shall be an hole in the top of it, in the midst thereof: it shall have a binding of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of an habergeon, that it be not rent.
verses.bsb: with an opening at its top in the center. Around the opening shall be a woven collar with an opening like that of a garment, so that it will not tear.

verses.kjv: ¶ And beneath upon the hem of it thou shalt make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, round about the hem thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:
verses.bsb: Make pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn all the way around the lower hem, with gold bells between them,

verses.kjv: A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the hem of the robe round about.
verses.bsb: alternating the gold bells and pomegranates around the lower hem of the robe.

verses.kjv: And it shall be upon Aaron to minister: and his sound shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy place before the Lord, and when he cometh out, that he die not.
verses.bsb: Aaron must wear the robe whenever he ministers, and its sound will be heard when he enters or exits the sanctuary before the LORD, so that he will not die.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and grave upon it, like the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.
verses.bsb: You are to make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it as on a seal:  HOLY TO THE LORD.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put it on a blue lace, that it may be upon the mitre; upon the forefront of the mitre it shall be.
verses.bsb: Fasten to it a blue cord to mount it on the turban; it shall be on the front of the turban.

verses.kjv: And it shall be upon Aaron’s forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the Lord.
verses.bsb: And it will be worn on Aaron’s forehead, so that he may bear the iniquity of the holy things that the sons of Israel consecrate with regard to all their holy gifts. It shall always be on his forehead, so that they may be acceptable before the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt embroider the coat of fine linen, and thou shalt make the mitre of fine linen, and thou shalt make the girdle of needlework.
verses.bsb: You are to weave the tunic with fine linen, make the turban of fine linen, and fashion an embroidered sash.

verses.kjv: ¶ And for Aaron’s sons thou shalt make coats, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and bonnets shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.
verses.bsb: Make tunics, sashes, and headbands for Aaron’s sons, to give them glory and splendor.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: After you put these garments on your brother Aaron and his sons, anoint them, ordain them, and consecrate them so that they may serve Me as priests.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their nakedness; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach:
verses.bsb: Make linen undergarments to cover their bare flesh, extending from waist to thigh.

verses.kjv: And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute for ever unto him and his seed after him.
verses.bsb: Aaron and his sons must wear them whenever they enter the Tent of Meeting or approach the altar to minister in the Holy Place, so that they will not incur guilt and die. This is to be a permanent statute for Aaron and his descendants.

verses.kjv: AND this is the thing that thou shalt do unto them to hallow them, to minister unto me in the priest’s office: Take one young bullock, and two rams without blemish,
verses.bsb: “Now this is what you are to do to consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve Me as priests: Take a young bull and two rams without blemish,

verses.kjv: And unleavened bread, and cakes unleavened tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil: of wheaten flour shalt thou make them.
verses.bsb: along with unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil. Make them out of fine wheat flour,

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.
verses.bsb: put them in a basket, and present them in the basket, along with the bull and the two rams.

verses.kjv: And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shalt wash them with water.
verses.bsb: Then present Aaron and his sons at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and wash them with water.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take the garments, and put upon Aaron the coat, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the curious girdle of the ephod:
verses.bsb: Take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself, and the breastplate. Fasten the ephod on him with its woven waistband.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and put the holy crown upon the mitre.
verses.bsb: Put the turban on his head and attach the holy diadem to the turban.

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour it upon his head, and anoint him.
verses.bsb: Then take the anointing oil and anoint him by pouring it on his head.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring his sons, and put coats upon them.
verses.bsb: Present his sons as well and clothe them with tunics.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and put the bonnets on them: and the priest’s office shall be theirs for a perpetual statute: and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.
verses.bsb: Wrap the sashes around Aaron and his sons and tie headbands on them. The priesthood shall be theirs by a permanent statute. In this way you are to ordain Aaron and his sons.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt cause a bullock to be brought before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the bullock.
verses.bsb: You are to present the bull at the front of the Tent of Meeting, and Aaron and his sons are to lay their hands on its head.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt kill the bullock before the Lord, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
verses.bsb: And you shall slaughter the bull before the LORD at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and pour all the blood beside the bottom of the altar.
verses.bsb: Take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; then pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul that is above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and burn them upon the altar.
verses.bsb: Take all the fat that covers the entrails and the lobe of the liver, and both kidneys with the fat on them, and burn them on the altar.

verses.kjv: But the flesh of the bullock, and his skin, and his dung, shalt thou burn with fire without the camp: it is a sin offering.
verses.bsb: But burn the flesh of the bull and its hide and dung outside the camp; it is a sin offering.
