verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt also take one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.
verses.bsb: Take one of the rams, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on its head.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt slay the ram, and thou shalt take his blood, and sprinkle it round about upon the altar.
verses.bsb: You are to slaughter the ram, take its blood, and sprinkle it on all sides of the altar.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and wash the inwards of him, and his legs, and put them unto his pieces, and unto his head.
verses.bsb: Cut the ram into pieces, wash the entrails and legs, and place them with its head and other pieces.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar: it is a burnt offering unto the Lord: it is a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then burn the entire ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the LORD, a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.
verses.bsb: Take the second ram, and Aaron and his sons are to lay their hands on its head.

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of his blood, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.
verses.bsb: Slaughter the ram, take some of its blood, and put it on the right earlobes of Aaron and his sons, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet. Sprinkle the remaining blood on all sides of the altar.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be hallowed, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.
verses.bsb: And take some of the blood on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and his garments, as well as on his sons and their garments. Then he and his garments will be consecrated, as well as his sons and their garments.

verses.kjv: Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat and the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right shoulder; for it is a ram of consecration:
verses.bsb: Take the fat from the ram, the fat tail, the fat covering the entrails, the lobe of the liver, both kidneys with the fat on them, and the right thigh (since this is a ram for ordination),

verses.kjv: And one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened bread that is before the Lord:
verses.bsb: along with one loaf of bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer from the basket of unleavened bread that is before the LORD.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put all in the hands of Aaron, and in the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them for a wave offering before the Lord.
verses.bsb: Put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons and wave them before the LORD as a wave offering.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt receive them of their hands, and burn them upon the altar for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour before the Lord: it is an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then take them from their hands and burn them on the altar atop the burnt offering as a pleasing aroma before the LORD; it is an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s consecration, and wave it for a wave offering before the Lord: and it shall be thy part.
verses.bsb: Take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s ordination and wave it before the LORD as a wave offering, and it will be your portion.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:
verses.bsb: Consecrate for Aaron and his sons the breast of the wave offering that is waved and the thigh of the heave offering that is lifted up from the ram of ordination.

verses.kjv: And it shall be Aaron’s and his sons’ by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, even their heave offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: This will belong to Aaron and his sons as a regular portion from the Israelites, for it is the heave offering the Israelites will make to the LORD from their peace offerings.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons’ after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.
verses.bsb: The holy garments that belong to Aaron will belong to his sons after him, so they can be anointed and ordained in them.

verses.kjv: And that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy place.
verses.bsb: The son who succeeds him as priest and enters the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place must wear them for seven days.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.
verses.bsb: You are to take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.

verses.kjv: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
verses.bsb: At the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, Aaron and his sons are to eat the meat of the ram and the bread that is in the basket.

verses.kjv: And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
verses.bsb: They must eat those things by which atonement was made for their ordination and consecration. But no outsider may eat them, because these things are sacred.

verses.kjv: And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
verses.bsb: And if any of the meat of ordination or any bread is left until the morning, you are to burn up the remainder. It must not be eaten, because it is sacred.

verses.kjv: And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all things which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.
verses.bsb: This is what you are to do for Aaron and his sons based on all that I have commanded you, taking seven days to ordain them.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.
verses.bsb: Sacrifice a bull as a sin offering each day for atonement. Purify the altar by making atonement for it, and anoint it to consecrate it.

verses.kjv: Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy: whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
verses.bsb: For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. Then the altar will become most holy; whatever touches the altar will be holy.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.
verses.bsb: This is what you are to offer regularly on the altar, each day: two lambs that are a year old.

verses.kjv: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:
verses.bsb: Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight.

verses.kjv: And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering.
verses.bsb: With the first lamb offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with a quarter hin of oil from pressed olives, and a drink offering of a quarter hin of wine.

verses.kjv: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: And offer the second lamb at twilight with the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning, as a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the Lord: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.
verses.bsb: For the generations to come, this burnt offering shall be made regularly at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the LORD, where I will meet you to speak with you.

verses.kjv: And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory.
verses.bsb: I will also meet with the Israelites there, and that place will be consecrated by My glory.

verses.kjv: And I will sanctify the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar: I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons, to minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: So I will consecrate the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and I will consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve Me as priests.

verses.kjv: ¶ And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
verses.bsb: Then I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.

verses.kjv: And they shall know that I am the Lord their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them: I am the Lord their God.
verses.bsb: And they will know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt so that I might dwell among them.  I am the LORD their God.

verses.kjv: AND thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it.
verses.bsb: “You are also to make an altar of acacia wood for the burning of incense.

verses.kjv: A cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same.
verses.bsb: It is to be square, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high. Its horns must be of one piece.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.
verses.bsb: Overlay with pure gold the top and all the sides and horns, and make a molding of gold around it.

verses.kjv: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make it; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal.
verses.bsb: And make two gold rings below the molding on opposite sides to hold the poles used to carry it.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
verses.bsb: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.
verses.bsb: Place the altar in front of the veil that is before the ark of the Testimony—before the mercy seat that is over the Testimony—where I will meet with you.

verses.kjv: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.
verses.bsb: And Aaron is to burn fragrant incense on it every morning when he tends the lamps.

verses.kjv: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the Lord throughout your generations.
verses.bsb: When Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight, he must burn the incense perpetually before the LORD for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.
verses.bsb: On this altar you must not offer unauthorized incense or a burnt offering or grain offering; nor are you to pour a drink offering on it.

verses.kjv: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Once a year Aaron shall make atonement on the horns of the altar. Throughout your generations he shall make atonement on it annually with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. The altar is most holy to the LORD.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the Lord, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them.
verses.bsb: “When you take a census of the Israelites to number them, each man must pay the LORD a ransom for his life when he is counted. Then no plague will come upon them when they are numbered.

verses.kjv: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel shall be the offering of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Everyone who crosses over to those counted must pay a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, which weighs twenty gerahs. This half shekel is an offering to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Everyone twenty years of age or older who crosses over must give this offering to the LORD.

verses.kjv: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
verses.bsb: In making the offering to the LORD to atone for your lives, the rich shall not give more than a half shekel, nor shall the poor give less.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the Lord, to make an atonement for your souls.
verses.bsb: Take the atonement money from the Israelites and use it for the service of the Tent of Meeting. It will serve as a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD to make atonement for your lives.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Thou shalt also make a laver of brass, and his foot also of brass, to wash withal: and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.
verses.bsb: “You are to make a bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. Set it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water in it,

verses.kjv: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:
verses.bsb: with which Aaron and his sons are to wash their hands and feet.

verses.kjv: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the Lord:
verses.bsb: Whenever they enter the Tent of Meeting or approach the altar to minister by presenting an offering made by fire to the LORD, they must wash with water so that they will not die.

verses.kjv: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations.
verses.bsb: Thus they are to wash their hands and feet so that they will not die; this shall be a permanent statute for Aaron and his descendants for the generations to come.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Moreover the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much, even two hundred and fifty shekels, and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty shekels,
verses.bsb: “Take the finest spices: 500 shekels of liquid myrrh, half that amount (250 shekels) of fragrant cinnamon, 250 shekels of fragrant cane,

verses.kjv: And of cassia five hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin:
verses.bsb: 500 shekels of cassia—all according to the sanctuary shekel—and a hin of olive oil.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.
verses.bsb: Prepare from these a sacred anointing oil, a fragrant blend, the work of a perfumer; it will be a sacred anointing oil.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,
verses.bsb: Use this oil to anoint the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the Testimony,

verses.kjv: And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense,
verses.bsb: the table and all its utensils, the lampstand and its utensils, the altar of incense,

verses.kjv: And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot.
verses.bsb: the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and the basin with its stand.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.
verses.bsb: You are to consecrate them so that they will be most holy. Whatever touches them shall be holy.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: Anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to serve Me as priests.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.
verses.bsb: And you are to tell the Israelites, ‘This will be My sacred anointing oil for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: Upon man’s flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you.
verses.bsb: It must not be used to anoint an ordinary man, and you must not make anything like it with the same formula. It is holy, and it must be holy to you.

verses.kjv: Whosoever compoundeth any like it, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.
verses.bsb: Anyone who mixes perfume like it or puts it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; these sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like weight:
verses.bsb: The LORD also said to Moses, “Take fragrant spices—gum resin, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense—in equal measures,

verses.kjv: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy:
verses.bsb: and make a fragrant blend of incense, the work of a perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.
verses.bsb: Grind some of it into fine powder and place it in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.

verses.kjv: And as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the Lord.
verses.bsb: You are never to use this formula to make incense for yourselves; you shall regard it as holy to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people.
verses.bsb: Anyone who makes something like it to enjoy its fragrance shall be cut off from his people.”

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:
verses.bsb: “See, I have called by name Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

verses.kjv: And I have filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship,
verses.bsb: And I have filled him with the Spirit of God, with skill, ability, and knowledge in all kinds of craftsmanship,

verses.kjv: To devise cunning works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,
verses.bsb: to design artistic works in gold, silver, and bronze,

verses.kjv: And in cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of timber, to work in all manner of workmanship.
verses.bsb: to cut gemstones for settings, and to carve wood, so that he may be a master of every craft.

verses.kjv: And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan: and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee;
verses.bsb: Moreover, I have selected Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, as his assistant.  I have also given skill to all the craftsmen, that they may fashion all that I have commanded you:

verses.kjv: The tabernacle of the congregation, and the ark of the testimony, and the mercy seat that is thereupon, and all the furniture of the tabernacle,
verses.bsb: the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the Testimony and the mercy seat upon it, and all the other furnishings of the tent—

verses.kjv: And the table and his furniture, and the pure candlestick with all his furniture, and the altar of incense,
verses.bsb: the table with its utensils, the pure gold lampstand with all its utensils, the altar of incense,

verses.kjv: And the altar of burnt offering with all his furniture, and the laver and his foot,
verses.bsb: the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand—

verses.kjv: And the cloths of service, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office,
verses.bsb: as well as the woven garments, both the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments for his sons to serve as priests,

verses.kjv: And the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the holy place: according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do.
verses.bsb: in addition to the anointing oil and fragrant incense for the Holy Place. They are to make them according to all that I have commanded you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that ye may know that I am the Lord that doth sanctify you.
verses.bsb: “Tell the Israelites, ‘Surely you must keep My Sabbaths, for this will be a sign between Me and you for the generations to come, so that you may know that I am the LORD who sanctifies you.

verses.kjv: Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it is holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
verses.bsb: Keep the Sabbath, for it is holy to you. Anyone who profanes it must surely be put to death. Whoever does any work on that day must be cut off from among his people.

verses.kjv: Six days may work be done; but in the seventh is the sabbath of rest, holy to the Lord: whosoever doeth any work in the sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: For six days work may be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the LORD. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day must surely be put to death.

verses.kjv: Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations, for a perpetual covenant.
verses.bsb: The Israelites must keep the Sabbath, celebrating it as a permanent covenant for the generations to come.

verses.kjv: It is a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever: for in six days the Lord made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.
verses.bsb: It is a sign between Me and the Israelites forever; for in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, but on the seventh day He rested and was refreshed.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he gave unto Moses, when he had made an end of communing with him upon mount Sinai, two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God.
verses.bsb: When the LORD had finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, He gave him the two tablets of the Testimony, tablets of stone inscribed by the finger of God.

verses.kjv: AND when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him, Up, make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
verses.bsb: Now when the people saw that Moses was delayed in coming down from the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and said, “Come, make us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him!”

verses.kjv: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them unto me.
verses.bsb: So Aaron told them, “Take off the gold earrings that are on your wives and sons and daughters, and bring them to me.”

verses.kjv: And all the people brake off the golden earrings which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.
verses.bsb: Then all the people took off their gold earrings and brought them to Aaron.

verses.kjv: And he received them at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, after he had made it a molten calf: and they said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: He took the gold from their hands, and with an engraving tool he fashioned it into a molten calf. And they said, “These, O Israel, are your gods, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!”

verses.kjv: And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation, and said, To morrow is a feast to the Lord.
verses.bsb: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar before the calf and proclaimed: “Tomorrow shall be a feast to the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.
verses.bsb: So the next day they arose, offered burnt offerings, and presented peace offerings. And the people sat down to eat and drink, and got up to indulge in revelry.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Go, get thee down; for thy people, which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go down at once, for your people, whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves.

verses.kjv: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them: they have made them a molten calf, and have worshipped it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: How quickly they have turned aside from the way that I commanded them! They have made for themselves a molten calf and have bowed down to it. They have sacrificed to it and said, ‘These, O Israel, are your gods, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, I have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people:
verses.bsb: The LORD also said to Moses, “I have seen this people, and they are indeed a stiff-necked people.

verses.kjv: Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may wax hot against them, and that I may consume them: and I will make of thee a great nation.
verses.bsb: Now leave Me alone, so that My anger may burn against them and consume them. Then I will make you into a great nation.”

verses.kjv: And Moses besought the Lord his God, and said, Lord, why doth thy wrath wax hot against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power, and with a mighty hand?
verses.bsb: But Moses sought the favor of the LORD his God, saying, “O LORD, why does Your anger burn against Your people, whom You brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and a mighty hand?

verses.kjv: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak, and say, For mischief did he bring them out, to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from thy fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against thy people.
verses.bsb: Why should the Egyptians declare, ‘He brought them out with evil intent, to kill them in the mountains and wipe them from the face of the earth’? Turn from Your fierce anger and relent from doing harm to Your people.

verses.kjv: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.
verses.bsb: Remember Your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, to whom You swore by Your very self when You declared, ‘I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all this land that I have promised, and it shall be their inheritance forever.’”

verses.kjv: And the Lord repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people.
verses.bsb: So the LORD relented from the calamity He had threatened to bring on His people.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses turned, and went down from the mount, and the two tables of the testimony were in his hand: the tables were written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other were they written.
verses.bsb: Then Moses turned and went down the mountain with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands. They were inscribed on both sides, front and back.

verses.kjv: And the tables were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, graven upon the tables.
verses.bsb: The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.

verses.kjv: And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses, There is a noise of war in the camp.
verses.bsb: When Joshua heard the sound of the people shouting, he said to Moses, “The sound of war is in the camp.”

verses.kjv: And he said, It is not the voice of them that shout for mastery, neither is it the voice of them that cry for being overcome: but the noise of them that sing do I hear.
verses.bsb: But Moses replied:  “It is neither the cry of victory nor the cry of defeat; I hear the sound of singing!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as soon as he came nigh unto the camp, that he saw the calf, and the dancing: and Moses’ anger waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and brake them beneath the mount.
verses.bsb: As Moses approached the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, he burned with anger and threw the tablets out of his hands, shattering them at the base of the mountain.

verses.kjv: And he took the calf which they had made, and burnt it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and strawed it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it.
verses.bsb: Then he took the calf they had made, burned it in the fire, ground it to powder, and scattered the powder over the face of the water. Then he forced the Israelites to drink it.

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?
verses.bsb: “What did this people do to you,” Moses asked Aaron, “that you have led them into so great a sin?”

verses.kjv: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord wax hot: thou knowest the people, that they are set on mischief.
verses.bsb: “Do not be enraged, my lord,” Aaron replied. “You yourself know that the people are intent on evil.

verses.kjv: For they said unto me, Make us gods, which shall go before us: for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
verses.bsb: They told me, ‘Make us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him!’

verses.kjv: And I said unto them, Whosoever hath any gold, let them break it off. So they gave it me: then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.
verses.bsb: So I said to them, ‘Whoever has gold, let him take it off,’ and they gave it to me. And when I threw it into the fire, out came this calf!”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when Moses saw that the people were naked; (for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame among their enemies:)
verses.bsb: Moses saw that the people were out of control, for Aaron had let them run wild and become a laughingstock to their enemies.

verses.kjv: Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, Who is on the Lord’s side? let him come unto me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him.
verses.bsb: So Moses stood at the entrance to the camp and said, “Whoever is for the LORD, come to me.”  And all the Levites gathered around him.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Put every man his sword by his side, and go in and out from gate to gate throughout the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbour.
verses.bsb: He told them, “This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: ‘Each of you men is to fasten his sword to his side, go back and forth through the camp from gate to gate, and slay his brother, his friend, and his neighbor.’”

verses.kjv: And the children of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.
verses.bsb: The Levites did as Moses commanded, and that day about three thousand of the people fell dead.

verses.kjv: For Moses had said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the Lord, even every man upon his son, and upon his brother; that he may bestow upon you a blessing this day.
verses.bsb: Afterward, Moses said, “Today you have been ordained for service to the LORD, since each man went against his son and his brother; so the LORD has bestowed a blessing on you this day.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye have sinned a great sin: and now I will go up unto the Lord; peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin.
verses.bsb: The next day Moses said to the people, “You have committed a great sin. Now I will go up to the LORD; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin.”

verses.kjv: And Moses returned unto the Lord, and said, Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made them gods of gold.
verses.bsb: So Moses returned to the LORD and said, “Oh, what a great sin these people have committed! They have made gods of gold for themselves.

verses.kjv: Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin—; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.
verses.bsb: Yet now, if You would only forgive their sin.... But if not, please blot me out of the book that You have written.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Whosoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.
verses.bsb: The LORD replied to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against Me, I will blot out of My book.

verses.kjv: Therefore now go, lead the people unto the place of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.
verses.bsb: Now go, lead the people to the place I described. Behold, My angel shall go before you. But on the day I settle accounts, I will punish them for their sin.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord plagued the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made.
verses.bsb: And the LORD sent a plague on the people because of what they had done with the calf that Aaron had made.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, Depart, and go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Leave this place, you and the people you brought up out of the land of Egypt, and go to the land that I promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob when I said, ‘I will give it to your descendants.’

verses.kjv: And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:
verses.bsb: And I will send an angel before you, and I will drive out the Canaanites, Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.

verses.kjv: Unto a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up in the midst of thee; for thou art a stiffnecked people: lest I consume thee in the way.
verses.bsb: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go with you, because you are a stiff-necked people; otherwise, I might destroy you on the way.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the people heard these evil tidings, they mourned: and no man did put on him his ornaments.
verses.bsb: When the people heard these bad tidings, they went into mourning, and no one put on any of his jewelry.

verses.kjv: For the Lord had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye are a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.
verses.bsb: For the LORD had said to Moses, “Tell the Israelites, ‘You are a stiff-necked people. If I should go with you for a single moment, I would destroy you. Now take off your jewelry, and I will decide what to do with you.’”

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments by the mount Horeb.
verses.bsb: So the Israelites stripped themselves of their jewelry from Mount Horeb onward.

verses.kjv: And Moses took the tabernacle, and pitched it without the camp, afar off from the camp, and called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. And it came to pass, that every one which sought the Lord went out unto the tabernacle of the congregation, which was without the camp.
verses.bsb: Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it at a distance outside the camp. He called it the Tent of Meeting, and anyone inquiring of the LORD would go to the Tent of Meeting outside the camp.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, that all the people rose up, and stood every man at his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Then, whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would stand at the entrances to their own tents and watch Moses until he entered the tent.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as Moses entered into the tabernacle, the cloudy pillar descended, and stood at the door of the tabernacle, and the Lord talked with Moses.
verses.bsb: As Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would come down and remain at the entrance, and the LORD would speak with Moses.

verses.kjv: And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand at the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man in his tent door.
verses.bsb: When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance to the tent, they would stand up and worship, each one at the entrance to his own tent.

verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And he turned again into the camp: but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not out of the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Thus the LORD would speak to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. Then Moses would return to the camp, but his young assistant Joshua son of Nun would not leave the tent.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the Lord, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to the LORD, “Look, You have been telling me, ‘Lead this people up,’ but You have not let me know whom You will send with me. Yet You have said, ‘I know you by name, and you have found favor in My sight.’

verses.kjv: Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation is thy people.
verses.bsb: Now if indeed I have found favor in Your sight, please let me know Your ways, that I may know You and find favor in Your sight. Remember that this nation is Your people.”

verses.kjv: And he said, My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.
verses.bsb: And the LORD answered, “My Presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, If thy presence go not with me, carry us not up hence.
verses.bsb: “If Your Presence does not go with us,” Moses replied, “do not lead us up from here.

verses.kjv: For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? is it not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that are upon the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: For how then can it be known that Your people and I have found favor in Your sight, unless You go with us? How else will we be distinguished from all the other people on the face of the earth?”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast spoken: for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name.
verses.bsb: So the LORD said to Moses, “I will do this very thing you have asked, for you have found favor in My sight, and I know you by name.”

verses.kjv: And he said, I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said, “Please show me Your glory.”

verses.kjv: And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thee, and I will proclaim the name of the Lord before thee; and will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.
verses.bsb: “I will cause all My goodness to pass before you,” the LORD replied, “and I will proclaim My name—the LORD—in your presence. I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live.
verses.bsb: But He added, “You cannot see My face, for no one can see Me and live.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Behold, there is a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock:
verses.bsb: The LORD continued, “There is a place near Me where you are to stand upon a rock,

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, while my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:
verses.bsb: and when My glory passes by, I will put you in a cleft of the rock and cover you with My hand until I have passed by.

verses.kjv: And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen.
verses.bsb: Then I will take My hand away, and you will see My back; but My face must not be seen.”

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first: and I will write upon these tables the words that were in the first tables, which thou brakest.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Chisel out two stone tablets like the originals, and I will write on them the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke.

verses.kjv: And be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning unto mount Sinai, and present thyself there to me in the top of the mount.
verses.bsb: Be ready in the morning, and come up on Mount Sinai to present yourself before Me on the mountaintop.

verses.kjv: And no man shall come up with thee, neither let any man be seen throughout all the mount; neither let the flocks nor herds feed before that mount.
verses.bsb: No one may go up with you; in fact, no one may be seen anywhere on the mountain—not even the flocks or herds may graze in front of the mountain.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he hewed two tables of stone like unto the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up unto mount Sinai, as the Lord had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tables of stone.
verses.bsb: So Moses chiseled out two stone tablets like the originals. He rose early in the morning, and taking the two stone tablets in his hands, he went up Mount Sinai as the LORD had commanded him.

verses.kjv: And the Lord descended in the cloud, and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And the LORD descended in a cloud, stood with him there, and proclaimed His name, the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the Lord passed by before him, and proclaimed, The Lord, The Lord God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD passed in front of Moses and called out:  “The LORD, the LORD God, is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion and faithfulness,

verses.kjv: Keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and that will by no means clear the guilty; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, and upon the children’s children, unto the third and to the fourth generation.
verses.bsb: maintaining loving devotion to a thousand generations, forgiving iniquity, transgression, and sin. Yet He will by no means leave the guilty unpunished; He will visit the iniquity of the fathers on their children and grandchildren to the third and fourth generations.”

verses.kjv: And Moses made haste, and bowed his head toward the earth, and worshipped.
verses.bsb: Moses immediately bowed down to the ground and worshiped.

verses.kjv: And he said, If now I have found grace in thy sight, O Lord, let my Lord, I pray thee, go among us; for it is a stiffnecked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for thine inheritance.
verses.bsb: “O Lord,” he said, “if I have indeed found favor in Your sight, my Lord, please go with us. Although this is a stiff-necked people, forgive our iniquity and sin, and take us as Your inheritance.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels, such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation: and all the people among which thou art shall see the work of the Lord: for it is a terrible thing that I will do with thee.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said, “Behold, I am making a covenant. Before all your people I will perform wonders that have never been done in any nation in all the world. All the people among whom you live will see the LORD’s work, for it is an awesome thing that I am doing with you.

verses.kjv: Observe thou that which I command thee this day: behold, I drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
verses.bsb: Observe what I command you this day. I will drive out before you the Amorites, Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.

verses.kjv: Take heed to thyself, lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land whither thou goest, lest it be for a snare in the midst of thee:
verses.bsb: Be careful not to make a treaty with the inhabitants of the land you are entering, lest they become a snare in your midst.

verses.kjv: But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves:
verses.bsb: Rather, you must tear down their altars, smash their sacred stones, and chop down their Asherah poles.

verses.kjv: For thou shalt worship no other god: for the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God:
verses.bsb: For you must not worship any other god, for the LORD, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.

verses.kjv: Lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they go a whoring after their gods, and do sacrifice unto their gods, and one call thee, and thou eat of his sacrifice;
verses.bsb: Do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, for when they prostitute themselves to their gods and sacrifice to them, they will invite you, and you will eat their sacrifices.

verses.kjv: And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after their gods, and make thy sons go a whoring after their gods.
verses.bsb: And when you take some of their daughters as brides for your sons, their daughters will prostitute themselves to their gods and cause your sons to do the same.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt make thee no molten gods.
verses.bsb: You shall make no molten gods for yourselves.

verses.kjv: ¶ The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep. Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, in the time of the month Abib: for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.
verses.bsb: You are to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib, you are to eat unleavened bread as I commanded you. For in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt.

verses.kjv: All that openeth the matrix is mine; and every firstling among thy cattle, whether ox or sheep, that is male.
verses.bsb: The first offspring of every womb belongs to Me, including all the firstborn males among your livestock, whether cattle or sheep.

verses.kjv: But the firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb: and if thou redeem him not, then shalt thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem. And none shall appear before me empty.
verses.bsb: You must redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb; but if you do not redeem it, you are to break its neck. You must redeem all the firstborn of your sons. No one shall appear before Me empty-handed.

verses.kjv: ¶ Six days thou shalt work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest: in earing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.
verses.bsb: Six days you shall labor, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even in the seasons of plowing and harvesting, you must rest.

verses.kjv: ¶ And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year’s end.
verses.bsb: And you are to celebrate the Feast of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.

verses.kjv: ¶ Thrice in the year shall all your men children appear before the Lord God, the God of Israel.
verses.bsb: Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.

verses.kjv: For I will cast out the nations before thee, and enlarge thy borders: neither shall any man desire thy land, when thou shalt go up to appear before the Lord thy God thrice in the year.
verses.bsb: For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders, and no one will covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD your God.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left unto the morning.
verses.bsb: Do not offer the blood of a sacrifice to Me along with anything leavened, and do not let any of the sacrifice from the Passover Feast remain until morning.

verses.kjv: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring unto the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
verses.bsb: Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the LORD your God.  You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Moses, Write thou these words: for after the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel.
verses.bsb: The LORD also said to Moses, “Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”

verses.kjv: And he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he did neither eat bread, nor drink water. And he wrote upon the tables the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.
verses.bsb: So Moses was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights without eating bread or drinking water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant—the Ten Commandments.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai with the two tables of testimony in Moses’ hand, when he came down from the mount, that Moses wist not that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.
verses.bsb: And when Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands, he was unaware that his face had become radiant from speaking with the LORD.

verses.kjv: And when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come nigh him.
verses.bsb: Aaron and all the Israelites looked at Moses, and behold, his face was radiant. And they were afraid to approach him.

verses.kjv: And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.
verses.bsb: But Moses called out to them; so Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke to them.

verses.kjv: And afterward all the children of Israel came nigh: and he gave them in commandment all that the Lord had spoken with him in mount Sinai.
verses.bsb: And after this all the Israelites came near, and Moses commanded them to do everything that the LORD had told him on Mount Sinai.

verses.kjv: And till Moses had done speaking with them, he put a vail on his face.
verses.bsb: When Moses had finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.

verses.kjv: But when Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he took the vail off, until he came out. And he came out, and spake unto the children of Israel that which he was commanded.
verses.bsb: But whenever Moses went in before the LORD to speak with Him, he would remove the veil until he came out. And when he came out, he would tell the Israelites what he had been commanded,

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses’ face shone: and Moses put the vail upon his face again, until he went in to speak with him.
verses.bsb: and the Israelites would see that the face of Moses was radiant. So Moses would put the veil back over his face until he went in to speak with the LORD.

verses.kjv: AND Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These are the words which the Lord hath commanded, that ye should do them.
verses.bsb: Then Moses assembled the whole congregation of Israel and said to them, “These are the things that the LORD has commanded you to do:

verses.kjv: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the Lord: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.
verses.bsb: For six days work may be done, but the seventh day shall be your holy day, a Sabbath of complete rest to the LORD. Whoever does any work on that day must be put to death.

verses.kjv: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: Do not light a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath day.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord commanded, saying,
verses.bsb: Moses also told the whole congregation of Israel, “This is what the LORD has commanded:

verses.kjv: Take ye from among you an offering unto the Lord: whosoever is of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the Lord; gold, and silver, and brass,
verses.bsb: Take from among you an offering to the LORD. Let everyone whose heart is willing bring an offering to the LORD:  gold, silver, and bronze;

verses.kjv: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair,
verses.bsb: blue, purple, and scarlet yarn;   fine linen and goat hair;

verses.kjv: And rams’ skins dyed red, and badgers’ skins, and shittim wood,
verses.bsb: ram skins dyed red and fine leather;   acacia wood;

verses.kjv: And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense,
verses.bsb: olive oil for the light;   spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;

verses.kjv: And onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate.
verses.bsb: and onyx stones and gemstones to be mounted on the ephod and breastpiece.

verses.kjv: And every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the Lord hath commanded;
verses.bsb: Let every skilled craftsman among you come and make everything that the LORD has commanded:

verses.kjv: The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets,
verses.bsb: the tabernacle with its tent and covering, its clasps and frames, its crossbars, posts, and bases;

verses.kjv: The ark, and the staves thereof, with the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering,
verses.bsb: the ark with its poles and mercy seat, and the veil to shield it;

verses.kjv: The table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread,
verses.bsb: the table with its poles, all its utensils, and the Bread of the Presence;

verses.kjv: The candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light,
verses.bsb: the lampstand for light with its accessories and lamps and oil for the light;

verses.kjv: And the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle,
verses.bsb: the altar of incense with its poles;   the anointing oil and fragrant incense;   the curtain for the doorway at the entrance to the tabernacle;

verses.kjv: The altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,
verses.bsb: the altar of burnt offering with its bronze grate, its poles, and all its utensils;   the basin with its stand;

verses.kjv: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court,
verses.bsb: the curtains of the courtyard with its posts and bases, and the curtain for the gate of the courtyard;

verses.kjv: The pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords,
verses.bsb: the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the courtyard, along with their ropes;

verses.kjv: The cloths of service, to do service in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office.
verses.bsb: and the woven garments for ministering in the holy place—both the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments for his sons to serve as priests.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.
verses.bsb: Then the whole congregation of Israel withdrew from the presence of Moses.

verses.kjv: And they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, and they brought the Lord’s offering to the work of the tabernacle of the congregation, and for all his service, and for the holy garments.
verses.bsb: And everyone whose heart stirred him and whose spirit prompted him came and brought an offering to the LORD for the work on the Tent of Meeting, for all its services, and for the holy garments.

verses.kjv: And they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, and brought bracelets, and earrings, and rings, and tablets, all jewels of gold: and every man that offered offered an offering of gold unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: So all who had willing hearts, both men and women, came and brought brooches and earrings, rings and necklaces, and all kinds of gold jewelry. And they all presented their gold as a wave offering to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats’ hair, and red skins of rams, and badgers’ skins, brought them.
verses.bsb: Everyone who had blue, purple, or scarlet yarn, or fine linen, goat hair, ram skins dyed red, or articles of fine leather, brought them.

verses.kjv: Every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the Lord’s offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought it.
verses.bsb: And all who could present an offering of silver or bronze brought it as a contribution to the LORD. Also, everyone who had acacia wood for any part of the service brought it.

verses.kjv: And all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, both of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, and of fine linen.
verses.bsb: Every skilled woman spun with her hands and brought what she had spun: blue, purple, or scarlet yarn, or fine linen.

verses.kjv: And all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats’ hair.
verses.bsb: And all the skilled women whose hearts were stirred spun the goat hair.

verses.kjv: And the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate;
verses.bsb: The leaders brought onyx stones and gemstones to mount on the ephod and breastpiece,

verses.kjv: And spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.
verses.bsb: as well as spices and olive oil for the light, for the anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.

verses.kjv: The children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the Lord, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work, which the Lord had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses.
verses.bsb: So all the men and women of the Israelites whose hearts prompted them brought a freewill offering to the LORD for all the work that the LORD through Moses had commanded them to do.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the Lord hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to the Israelites, “See, the LORD has called by name Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

verses.kjv: And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship;
verses.bsb: And He has filled him with the Spirit of God, with skill, ability, and knowledge in all kinds of craftsmanship,

verses.kjv: And to devise curious works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,
verses.bsb: to design artistic works in gold, silver, and bronze,

verses.kjv: And in the cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of wood, to make any manner of cunning work.
verses.bsb: to cut gemstones for settings, and to carve wood, so that he may be a master of every artistic craft.

verses.kjv: And he hath put in his heart that he may teach, both he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.
verses.bsb: And the LORD has given both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, the ability to teach others.

verses.kjv: Them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, even of them that do any work, and of those that devise cunning work.
verses.bsb: He has filled them with skill to do all kinds of work as engravers, designers, embroiderers in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen, and as weavers—as artistic designers of every kind of craft.

verses.kjv: THEN wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whom the Lord put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that the Lord had commanded.
verses.bsb: “So Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled person are to carry out everything commanded by the LORD, who has given them skill and ability to know how to perform all the work of constructing the sanctuary.”

verses.kjv: And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whose heart the Lord had put wisdom, even every one whose heart stirred him up to come unto the work to do it:
verses.bsb: Then Moses summoned Bezalel, Oholiab, and every skilled person whom the LORD had gifted—everyone whose heart stirred him to come and do the work.

verses.kjv: And they received of Moses all the offering, which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it withal. And they brought yet unto him free offerings every morning.
verses.bsb: They received from Moses all the contributions that the Israelites had brought to carry out the service of constructing the sanctuary.  Meanwhile, the people continued to bring freewill offerings morning after morning,

verses.kjv: And all the wise men, that wrought all the work of the sanctuary, came every man from his work which they made;
verses.bsb: so that all the skilled craftsmen who were doing all the work on the sanctuary left their work

verses.kjv: ¶ And they spake unto Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work, which the Lord commanded to make.
verses.bsb: and said to Moses, “The people are bringing more than enough for doing the work the LORD has commanded us to do.”

verses.kjv: And Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the offering of the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.
verses.bsb: After Moses had given an order, they sent a proclamation throughout the camp: “No man or woman should make anything else as an offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing more,

verses.kjv: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.
verses.bsb: since what they already had was more than enough to perform all the work.

verses.kjv: ¶ And every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: with cherubims of cunning work made he them.
verses.bsb: All the skilled craftsmen among the workmen made the ten curtains for the tabernacle. They were made of finely spun linen, as well as blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, with cherubim skillfully worked into them.

verses.kjv: The length of one curtain was twenty and eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: the curtains were all of one size.
verses.bsb: Each curtain was twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide; all the curtains were the same size.

verses.kjv: And he coupled the five curtains one unto another: and the other five curtains he coupled one unto another.
verses.bsb: And he joined five of the curtains together, and the other five he joined as well.

verses.kjv: And he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling: likewise he made in the uttermost side of another curtain, in the coupling of the second.
verses.bsb: He made loops of blue material on the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and also on the end curtain in the second set.

verses.kjv: Fifty loops made he in one curtain, and fifty loops made he in the edge of the curtain which was in the coupling of the second: the loops held one curtain to another.
verses.bsb: He made fifty loops on one curtain and fifty loops on the end curtain of the second set, so that the loops lined up opposite one another.

verses.kjv: And he made fifty taches of gold, and coupled the curtains one unto another with the taches: so it became one tabernacle.
verses.bsb: He also made fifty gold clasps to join the curtains together, so that the tabernacle was a unit.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made curtains of goats’ hair for the tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains he made them.
verses.bsb: He then made curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven curtains in all.

verses.kjv: The length of one curtain was thirty cubits, and four cubits was the breadth of one curtain: the eleven curtains were of one size.
verses.bsb: Each of the eleven curtains was the same size—thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.

verses.kjv: And he coupled five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.
verses.bsb: He joined five of the curtains into one set and the other six into another.

verses.kjv: And he made fifty loops upon the uttermost edge of the curtain in the coupling, and fifty loops made he upon the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.
verses.bsb: He made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set.

verses.kjv: And he made fifty taches of brass to couple the tent together, that it might be one.
verses.bsb: He also made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together as a unit.

verses.kjv: And he made a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of badgers’ skins above that.
verses.bsb: Additionally, he made for the tent a covering of ram skins dyed red, and over that a covering of fine leather.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood, standing up.
verses.bsb: Next, he constructed upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.

verses.kjv: The length of a board was ten cubits, and the breadth of a board one cubit and a half.
verses.bsb: Each frame was ten cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.

verses.kjv: One board had two tenons, equally distant one from another: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
verses.bsb: Two tenons were connected to each other for each frame. He made all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.

verses.kjv: And he made boards for the tabernacle; twenty boards for the south side southward:
verses.bsb: He constructed twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.
verses.bsb: with forty silver bases to put under the twenty frames—two bases for each frame, one under each tenon.

verses.kjv: And for the other side of the tabernacle, which is toward the north corner, he made twenty boards,
verses.bsb: For the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty frames

verses.kjv: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
verses.bsb: and forty silver bases—two bases under each frame.

verses.kjv: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.
verses.bsb: He made six frames for the rear of the tabernacle, the west side,

verses.kjv: And two boards made he for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.
verses.bsb: and two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And they were coupled beneath, and coupled together at the head thereof, to one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners.
verses.bsb: coupled together from bottom to top and fitted into a single ring. He made both corners in this way.

verses.kjv: And there were eight boards; and their sockets were sixteen sockets of silver, under every board two sockets.
verses.bsb: So there were eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
verses.bsb: He also made five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle,

verses.kjv: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward.
verses.bsb: five for those on the other side, and five for those on the rear side of the tabernacle, to the west.

verses.kjv: And he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other.
verses.bsb: He made the central crossbar to run through the center of the frames, from one end to the other.

verses.kjv: And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold to be places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.
verses.bsb: And he overlaid the frames with gold and made gold rings to hold the crossbars. He also overlaid the crossbars with gold.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made a vail of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: with cherubims made he it of cunning work.
verses.bsb: Next, he made the veil of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen, with cherubim skillfully worked into it.

verses.kjv: And he made thereunto four pillars of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold: their hooks were of gold; and he cast for them four sockets of silver.
verses.bsb: He also made four posts of acacia wood for it and overlaid them with gold, along with gold hooks; and he cast four silver bases for the posts.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made an hanging for the tabernacle door of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, of needlework;
verses.bsb: For the entrance to the tent, he made a curtain embroidered with blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and finely spun linen,

verses.kjv: And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets were of brass.
verses.bsb: together with five posts and their hooks.  He overlaid the tops of the posts and their bands with gold, and their five bases were bronze.

verses.kjv: AND Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half was the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it:
verses.bsb: Bezalel went on to construct the ark of acacia wood, two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and a cubit and a half high.

verses.kjv: And he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about.
verses.bsb: He overlaid it with pure gold, both inside and out, and made a gold molding around it.

verses.kjv: And he cast for it four rings of gold, to be set by the four corners of it; even two rings upon the one side of it, and two rings upon the other side of it.
verses.bsb: And he cast four gold rings for its four feet, two rings on one side and two on the other.

verses.kjv: And he made staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.
verses.bsb: Then he made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.

verses.kjv: And he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.
verses.bsb: He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry it.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made the mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half was the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof.
verses.bsb: He constructed a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.

verses.kjv: And he made two cherubims of gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat;
verses.bsb: He made two cherubim of hammered gold at the ends of the mercy seat,

verses.kjv: One cherub on the end on this side, and another cherub on the other end on that side: out of the mercy seat made he the cherubims on the two ends thereof.
verses.bsb: one cherub on one end and one on the other, all made from one piece of gold.

verses.kjv: And the cherubims spread out their wings on high, and covered with their wings over the mercy seat, with their faces one to another; even to the mercy seatward were the faces of the cherubims.
verses.bsb: And the cherubim had wings that spread upward, overshadowing the mercy seat. The cherubim faced each other, looking toward the mercy seat.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made the table of shittim wood: two cubits was the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof:
verses.bsb: He also made the table of acacia wood two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.

verses.kjv: And he overlaid it with pure gold, and made thereunto a crown of gold round about.
verses.bsb: He overlaid it with pure gold and made a gold molding around it.

verses.kjv: Also he made thereunto a border of an handbreadth round about; and made a crown of gold for the border thereof round about.
verses.bsb: And he made a rim around it a handbreadth wide and put a gold molding on the rim.

verses.kjv: And he cast for it four rings of gold, and put the rings upon the four corners that were in the four feet thereof.
verses.bsb: He cast four gold rings for the table and fastened them to the four corners at its four legs.

verses.kjv: Over against the border were the rings, the places for the staves to bear the table.
verses.bsb: The rings were placed close to the rim, to serve as holders for the poles used to carry the table.

verses.kjv: And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold, to bear the table.
verses.bsb: He made the poles of acacia wood for carrying the table and overlaid them with gold.

verses.kjv: And he made the vessels which were upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold.
verses.bsb: He also made the utensils for the table out of pure gold: its plates and dishes, as well as its bowls and pitchers for pouring drink offerings.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same:
verses.bsb: Then he made the lampstand out of pure hammered gold, all of one piece: its base and shaft, its cups, and its buds and petals.

verses.kjv: And six branches going out of the sides thereof; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof:
verses.bsb: Six branches extended from the sides, three on one side and three on the other.

verses.kjv: Three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower; and three bowls made like almonds in another branch, a knop and a flower: so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick.
verses.bsb: There were three cups shaped like almond blossoms on the first branch, each with buds and petals, three on the next branch, and the same for all six branches that extended from the lampstand.

verses.kjv: And in the candlestick were four bowls made like almonds, his knops, and his flowers:
verses.bsb: And on the lampstand were four cups shaped like almond blossoms with buds and petals.
